Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act and other Acts and to make certain consequential amendments
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act and other Acts and to make certain consequential amendments

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Elections Act to establish spending limits for third parties and political parties during a defined period before the election period of a general election held on a day fixed under that Act. It also establishes measures to increase transparency regarding the participation of third parties in the electoral process. Among other things that it does in this regard, the enactment

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: adds reporting requirements for third parties engaging in partisan activities, partisan advertising, and election surveys to the reporting requirements for third parties engaging in election advertising;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: creates an obligation for third parties to open a separate bank account for expenses related to the matters referred to in paragraph (a); and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: creates an obligation for political parties and third parties to identify themselves in partisan advertising during the defined period before the election period.
The enactment also amends the Act to implement measures to reduce barriers to participation and increase accessibility. Among other things that it does in this regard, the enactment

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: establishes a Register of Future Electors in which Canadian citizens 14 to 17 years of age may consent to be included;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: broadens the application of accommodation measures to all persons with a disability, irrespective of its nature;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: creates a financial incentive for registered parties and candidates to take steps to accommodate persons with a disability during an election period;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: amends some of the rules regarding the treatment of candidates’ expenses, including the rules related to childcare expenses, expenses related to the care of a person with a disability and litigation expenses;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: amends the rules regarding the treatment of nomination contestants’ and leadership contestants’ litigation expenses and personal expenses;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: allows Canadian Forces electors access to several methods of voting, while also adopting measures to ensure the integrity of the vote;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: removes limitations on public education and information activities conducted by the Chief Electoral Officer;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: removes two limitations on voting by non-resident electors: the requirement that they have been residing outside Canada for less than five consecutive years and the requirement that they intend to return to Canada to resume residence in the future; and

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: extends voting hours on advance polling days.

The enactment also amends the Act to modernize voting services, facilitate enforcement and improve various aspects of the administration of elections and of political financing. Among other things that it does in this regard, the enactment

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: removes the assignment of specific responsibilities set out in the Act to specific election officers by creating a generic category of election officer to whom all those responsibilities may be assigned;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: limits election periods to a maximum of 50 days;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: removes administrative barriers in order to facilitate the hiring of election officers;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: authorizes the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration to provide the Chief Electoral Officer with information about permanent residents and foreign nationals for the purpose of updating the Register of Electors;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: removes the prohibition on the Chief Electoral Officer authorizing the notice of confirmation of registration (commonly known as a “voter information card”) as identification;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: replaces, in the context of voter identification, the option of attestation for residence with an option of vouching for identity and residence;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: removes the requirement for electors’ signatures during advance polls, changes procedures for the closing of advance polls and allows for counting ballots from advance polls one hour before the regular polls close;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: replaces the right or obligation to take an oath with a right or obligation to make a solemn declaration, and streamlines the various declarations that electors may have the right or obligation to make under specific circumstances;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: relocates the Commissioner of Canada Elections to within the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer, and provides that the Commissioner is to be appointed by the Chief Electoral Officer, after consultation with the Director of Public Prosecutions, for a non-renewable term of 10 years;

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: provides the Commissioner of Canada Elections with the authority to impose administrative monetary penalties for contraventions of provisions of Parts 16, 17 and 18 of the Act and certain other provisions of the Act;

Provision.Label: (k)
Provision.Text: provides the Commissioner of Canada Elections with the authority to lay charges;

Provision.Label: (l)
Provision.Text: provides the Commissioner of Canada Elections with the power to apply for a court order requiring testimony or a written return;

Provision.Label: (m)
Provision.Text: clarifies offences relating to

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: the publishing of false statements,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: participation by non-Canadians in elections, including inducing electors to vote or refrain from voting, and

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: impersonation; and

Provision.Label: (n)
Provision.Text: implements a number of measures to harmonize and streamline political financing monitoring and reporting.

The enactment also amends the Act to provide for certain requirements with regard to the protection of personal information for registered parties, eligible parties and political parties that are applying to become registered parties, including the obligation for the party to adopt a policy for the protection of personal information and to publish it on its Internet site.

The enactment also amends the Parliament of Canada Act to prevent the calling of a by-election when a vacancy in the House of Commons occurs within nine months before the day fixed for a general election under the Canada Elections Act.

It also amends the Public Service Employment Act to clarify that the maximum period of employment of casual workers in the Office of the Chief Electoral Officer — 165 working days in one calendar year — applies to those who are appointed by the Commissioner of Canada Elections.
Finally, the enactment contains transitional provisions, makes consequential amendments to other Acts and repeals the Special Voting Rules.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Elections Modernization Act.

Subsection.Text: The definitions leadership campaign expense and nomination campaign expense in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Elections Act are repealed.
The definitions capital asset, election officer, polling day, prescribed and Register of Electors in subsection 2(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: capital asset means any property with a commercial value of more than $200 that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a registered party, an electoral district association or a candidate, is normally used outside an election period other than for the purposes of an election;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a nomination contestant, is normally used outside a nomination contest other than for the purposes of a nomination contest; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a leadership contestant, is normally used outside a leadership contest other than for the purposes of a leadership contest. (bien immobilisé)

Definition.Text: election officer means a person referred to in subsection 22(1) or appointed under section 32. (fonctionnaire électoral)
polling day, in relation to an election, means the date fixed for voting at the election under paragraph 57(1.2)(c) or subsection 59(4) or 77(2). (jour du scrutin)
prescribed, in relation to a form or a solemn declaration, means one that is authorized by the Chief Electoral Officer. (prescrit)
Register of Electors means the Register of Electors established under paragraph 44(1)(a). (Registre des électeurs)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition election documents in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the nomination papers and other documents filed by the candidates or filed on their behalf under section 67;

Subsection.Text: The definition election documents in subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (e) and by replacing paragraphs (f) and (g) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the other documents sent to the Chief Electoral Officer from the various polling stations, the various advance polling stations or the returning officer’s office, including

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: stubs and unused ballot papers,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: ballot papers cast for the various candidates,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: spoiled ballot papers,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: rejected ballot papers,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the list of electors used at the polling station, advance polling station or returning officer’s office,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: written authorizations of candidates’ representatives,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: used transfer certificates, if any, and

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: registration certificates; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the prescribed forms referred to in section 162 — other than those referred to in paragraph 162(i.1) — and any other prescribed form to be used at a polling station, advance polling station or returning officer’s office that contains personal information relating to an elector. (documents électoraux)

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the definition spoiled in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one that has not been deposited in the ballot box but has been found by an election officer or a unit election officer as defined in section 177 to be soiled or improperly printed; or

Subsection.Text: The definition appartenance politique in subsection 2(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: appartenance politique S’agissant d’un candidat, la désignation du parti politique qui le soutient ou la désignation « indépendant(e) », selon le cas, mentionnée dans son acte de candidature conformément au sous-alinéa 66(1)a)(v). (political affiliation)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: election advertising means the transmission to the public by any means during an election period of an advertising message that promotes or opposes a registered party or the election of a candidate, including by taking a position on an issue with which a registered party or candidate is associated. For greater certainty, it does not include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission to the public of an editorial, a debate, a speech, an interview, a column, a letter, a commentary or news;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the distribution of a book, or the promotion of the sale of a book, for no less than its commercial value, if the book was planned to be made available to the public regardless of whether there was to be an election;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of a document directly by a person or a group to their members, employees or shareholders, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission by an individual, on a non-commercial basis on the Internet, of his or her personal political views; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the making of telephone calls to electors only to encourage them to vote. (publicité électorale)

Definition.Text: election advertising expense means an expense incurred in relation to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the production of an election advertising message; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of an election advertising message to the public. (dépenses de publicité électorale)

Definition.Text: election survey means a survey respecting whether persons intend to vote at an election or who they voted for or will vote for at an election or respecting an issue with which a registered party or candidate is associated. (sondage électoral)
future elector means a Canadian citizen who is 14 years of age or older but under 18 years of age. (futur électeur)
National Capital Region means the National Capital Region as described in the schedule to the National Capital Act. (région de la capitale nationale)

partisan advertising means the transmission to the public by any means during a pre-election period of an advertising message that promotes or opposes a registered party or eligible party or the election of a potential candidate, nomination contestant or leader of a registered party or eligible party, otherwise than by taking a position on an issue with which any such party or person is associated. For greater certainty, it does not include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission to the public of an editorial, a debate, a speech, an interview, a column, a letter, a commentary or news;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the distribution of a book, or the promotion of the sale of a book, for no less than its commercial value, if the book was planned to be made available to the public regardless of whether there was to be an election;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of a document by a Senator or a member the expense of which is paid by the Senate or House of Commons;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of a document directly by a person or a group to their members, employees or shareholders, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission by an individual, on a non-commercial basis on the Internet, of his or her personal political views; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the making of telephone calls to electors only to encourage them to vote. (publicité partisane)

Definition.Text: partisan advertising expense means an expense incurred in relation to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the production of a partisan advertising message; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of a partisan advertising message to the public. (dépenses de publicité partisane)

Definition.Text: potential candidate means a person whose nomination as a candidate at an election has not been confirmed under subsection 71(1) but who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is selected in a nomination contest;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is deemed to be a candidate under section 477;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is a member or, if Parliament is dissolved, was a member on the day before the dissolution; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has the support of a political party to be a candidate of that party. (candidat potentiel)

Definition.Text: pre-election period means the period beginning on the June 30 before the day set in accordance with subsection 56.1(2) for the holding of a general election and ending on the day before the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the first day of an election period for a general election, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the 37th day before the Monday referred to in subsection 56.1(2) or, if the Governor in Council makes an order under subsection 56.2(3), the 37th day before the alternate day referred to in that order. (période préélectorale)

Definition.Text: Register of Future Electors means the Register of Future Electors established under paragraph 44(1)(b). (Registre des futurs électeurs)

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 2(1.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of this Act, the commercial value of any capital asset that is used during a nomination contest, an election period or a leadership contest, as the case may be, is the lower of
Subsection 2(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Satisfactory proof of identity and residence
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this Act, satisfactory proof of identity and satisfactory proof of residence are established in the manner determined by the Chief Electoral Officer.
Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Definitions of election advertising and partisan advertising
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the definitions election advertising and partisan advertising, promoting or opposing includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to a registered party or eligible party,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: naming it,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: identifying it, including by its logo, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: providing a link to an Internet page that does anything referred to in subparagraphs (i) and (ii); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to the election of a potential candidate, a nomination contestant, a candidate or a leader of a registered party or eligible party,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: naming him or her,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: showing a photograph, cartoon or drawing of him or her,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: identifying him or her, including by political affiliation or by any logo that he or she has, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: providing a link to an Internet page that does anything referred to in subparagraphs (i) to (iii).

Section.Text: Sections 3 to 5 of the Act are replaced by the following:
Every person who is a Canadian citizen and who on polling day is 18 years of age or older is qualified as an elector.
Section 7 of the Act is repealed.
Section 8 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The place of ordinary residence of a person who resides outside Canada is their last place of ordinary residence in Canada.
The portion of section 10 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Former member candidates and electors living with them

Subsection.Text: Each candidate at a general election who, on the day before the dissolution of Parliament immediately before the election, was a member, and any elector living with the candidate on that day who would move, or has moved, with the candidate to continue to live with the candidate, is entitled to have his or her name entered on the list of electors for, and to vote at the polling station to which is assigned, the polling division in which is located
Subsection 10(1) is amended by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (c) and by repealing paragraphs (b) and (d).
Section 10 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Former member candidates and electors living with them — notice to returning officer
Subsection.Text: If a candidate or elector referred to in subsection (1) provides notice to the returning officer, at least two days before polling day, of his or her intention to vote at the polling station, the candidate or elector is entitled to have his or her name entered on the list of electors for, and to vote at the polling station to which is assigned, any polling division in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the electoral district in which the former member is a candidate; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the electoral district in the National Capital Region where the former member resides for the purpose of carrying out parliamentary duties.

Section.Text: Section 11 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 15(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections 509.1(2) and (3), the Chief Electoral Officer shall rank as and have all the powers of a deputy head of a department, shall perform the duties of the office on a full-time basis and shall not hold any other office under Her Majesty or engage in any other employment.
Subsection 16.1(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Before issuing a guideline or interpretation note, the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide a copy of the proposed guideline or interpretation note to the Commissioner and to the members of the Advisory Committee of Political Parties established by subsection 21.1(1). The Commissioner and those members may provide their written comments to the Chief Electoral Officer within 45 days after the day on which the copy is sent.
Subsections 16.1(5) to (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer shall issue the guideline or interpretation note by registering it in the registry referred to in section 16.4 as soon as possible after preparing it.
Subsection 16.2(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Before issuing an opinion, the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide a copy of the proposed opinion to the Commissioner and to the members of the Advisory Committee of Political Parties established by subsection 21.1(1). The Commissioner and those members may provide their written comments to the Chief Electoral Officer within 30 days after the day on which the copy is sent.
Subsection 16.2(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Within 90 days after the day on which the application is made, the Chief Electoral Officer shall publish on his or her Internet site for a period of 30 days the opinion as well as a notice stating that the opinion will be issued at the expiry of that period. However, if the 90-day period coincides or overlaps with the election period of a general election, the opinion and the notice shall be published no later than 90 days after polling day for that election.

Section.Text: Section 16.3 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an opinion that is published under subsection 16.2(4) interprets a provision of the Act in a way that contradicts an interpretation of that provision provided in a previously issued opinion, the new interpretation does not replace the former interpretation until the date that the new opinion is issued under section 16.2.
Subsection 16.5(1) of the Act is repealed.
Section 17.1 of the Act is repealed.
Subsections 18(1) to (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may implement public education and information programs to make the electoral process better known to the public, particularly to those persons and groups most likely to experience difficulties in exercising their democratic rights.

The Chief Electoral Officer may, using any media or other means that he or she considers appropriate, provide the public, both inside and outside Canada, with information relating to Canada’s electoral process and the democratic rights to vote and to be a candidate.
The Chief Electoral Officer may establish programs to disseminate information outside Canada concerning how to vote under Part 11.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Communication with electors with a disability
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall ensure that any information on the following that is provided under subsections (1) to (1.2) in an advertising message is accessible to electors with a disability:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: how to become a candidate;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may have their name added to a list of electors and may have corrections made to information respecting them on the list;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may vote under section 127 and the times, dates and locations for voting;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: how an elector may prove their identity and residence in order to vote, including the pieces of identification that they may use to that end; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the measures for assisting electors with a disability to access a polling station or advance polling station or to mark a ballot.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Communication with future electors with a disability
Subsection.Text: If the Chief Electoral Officer provides, in an advertising message, any information under subsections (1) and (1.1) on how a future elector may have their name added to the Register of Future Electors and may have corrections made to information respecting them in that register, the Chief Electoral Officer shall ensure that the information is accessible to future electors with a disability.

Section.Text: Sections 18.01 and 18.1 of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer may provide assistance and cooperation in electoral matters to electoral agencies in other countries or to international organizations.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may carry out studies on voting, including studies respecting alternative voting means.
The Chief Electoral Officer may devise and test an alternative voting process for future use in an election.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Voting technology — electors with a disability
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall develop, obtain or adapt voting technology for use by electors with a disability, and may test the technology for future use in an election.

Neither an alternative voting process nor voting technology tested under subsection (2) or (3) may be used in an election without the prior approval of the committees of the Senate and of the House of Commons that normally consider electoral matters.

Section.Text: Subsection 18.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Power to enter into contracts, etc.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may enter into contracts, memoranda of understanding or other arrangements in the name of Her Majesty in right of Canada or in the Chief Electoral Officer’s name in the exercise or performance of his or her powers, duties and functions under this Act or any other Act of Parliament.

Section.Text: Section 19 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:
The staff of the Chief Electoral Officer consists of employees appointed in accordance with the Public Service Employment Act.
Subsection 20(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Any additional employees that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary for the exercise or performance of his or her powers, duties and functions under this Act that are related to the preparation for, and the conduct of, an election may be employed on a casual or temporary basis in accordance with the Public Service Employment Act.

Section.Text: Section 21 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chief Electoral Officer may delegate to any member of his or her staff, subject to any limitations that he or she may specify, any of his or her powers, duties and functions under this or any other Act of Parliament, except the power to delegate.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 22(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: persons authorized by a returning officer under section 27 to exercise powers or perform duties under this Act;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 22(1)(d) to (k) of the Act are repealed.
Subsection 22(1) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (m), by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (n) and by repealing paragraph (o).
Paragraph 22(3)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a person who, within seven years before the proposed appointment, has been found guilty of any offence under this Act or the Referendum Act, or a regulation made under the Referendum Act, or under an Act of the legislature of a province, or a regulation made under an Act of the legislature of a province, relating to provincial, municipal or school board elections.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 22(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

An election officer must be qualified as an elector, and an election officer referred to in paragraph (1)(a.1) or (b) must reside in the electoral district in which he or she is to exercise powers or perform duties under this Act or in an adjacent electoral district.
Despite subsection (4), an election officer appointed under section 32 may be under 18 years of age but must be at least 16 years of age.

Section.Text: Section 23 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Before assuming duties, an election officer shall make, in writing, a solemn declaration, in the prescribed form, that he or she will exercise the powers and perform the duties of the office in an impartial manner.

No election officer shall communicate information, or use personal information, that is obtained in the course of exercising his or her powers or performing his or her duties under this Act, other than for a purpose related to the exercise of those powers or the performance of those duties.
The returning officer shall send his or her solemn declaration and that of the assistant returning officer without delay to the Chief Electoral Officer.
Paragraphs 23.2(9)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is incapable, by reason of illness, disability or otherwise, of satisfactorily exercising their powers or performing their duties under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fails to competently exercise a field liaison officer’s powers or competently perform a field liaison officer’s duties under this Act or to comply with an instruction of the Chief Electoral Officer issued under paragraph 16(c); or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 23.2(9)(c) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection (8), whether or not the contravention occurs in the exercise of their powers or the performance of their duties under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 24(7)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is incapable, by reason of illness, disability or otherwise, of satisfactorily exercising his or her powers or performing his or her duties under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fails to competently exercise a returning officer’s power or competently perform a returning officer’s duty under this Act or to comply with an instruction of the Chief Electoral Officer described in paragraph 16(c);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 24(7)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection (6), whether or not the contravention occurs in the exercise of his or her powers or the performance of his or her duties under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 24(8) and (9) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer may temporarily suspend from office a returning officer for any grounds set out in subsection (7).

The period of suspension expires at the end of the period that the Chief Electoral Officer considers appropriate. However, if a procedure is commenced — either before or during the period of suspension — that could lead to the returning officer’s removal, the period of suspension expires on the day on which the Chief Electoral Officer makes his or her final decision in that regard.

Section.Text: Subsection 26(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Without delay after being appointed, a returning officer shall, with the Chief Electoral Officer’s prior approval of the individual proposed to be appointed, appoint an assistant returning officer, who shall hold office at pleasure.

Section.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The returning officer for an electoral district may, with the Chief Electoral Officer’s prior approval, authorize any person acting under his or her direction to exercise any of the powers or perform any of the duties of a returning officer under this Act, except those described in subsection 24(3), sections 62 and 63, subsection 71(1), sections 71.1, 74, 77, 130, 293 to 298 and 300, subsection 301(6) and sections 313 to 316.

Section.Text: Subsections 28(3.01) and (3.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Designated person to act — suspension of returning officer
Subsection.Text: If a returning officer is under suspension during the six months before the day set in accordance with subsection 56.1(2) for the holding of a general election or during any election period that falls in whole or in part outside that six-month period, the Chief Electoral Officer may designate a person to act in the returning officer’s place, and that person may, during and after that period, exercise the powers and perform the duties of a returning officer in relation to that election.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Designated person to act — absence of returning and assistant returning officers
Subsection.Text: If, during the six months before the day set in accordance with subsection 56.1(2) for the holding of a general election or during any election period that falls in whole or in part outside that six-month period, a returning officer is absent or unable to act or a returning officer’s office is vacant and, at the same time, the assistant returning officer is absent or unable to act or the assistant returning officer’s office is vacant, the Chief Electoral Officer shall designate a person to act in place of the returning officer, and that person may, during and after that period, exercise the powers and perform the duties of a returning officer in relation to that election.
Subsection 29(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an assistant returning officer dies, resigns, becomes disqualified or incapable of acting or refuses to act, or is removed from office for any other reason, the returning officer shall without delay appoint a substitute with the Chief Electoral Officer’s prior approval.
Subsection 29(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An assistant returning officer who intends to resign shall give written notice to the returning officer or, if the office of returning officer is vacant, to the Chief Electoral Officer.

Section.Text: Subsection 30(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An assistant returning officer appointed under subsection (2) is not an assistant returning officer for the purposes of subsections 28(1), 60(2), 70(1) and 293(1).

Section.Text: The heading “General” before section 32 and sections 32 to 39 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) and (3) and 33(2) and (3), a returning officer shall, after the issue of the writ, appoint in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions the election officers that the returning officer considers necessary for exercising election officers’ powers and performing election officers’ duties under this Act in the returning officer’s electoral district.

A returning officer may, in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions, appoint election officers before the issue of the writ if the returning officer considers that it is desirable to do so for the purpose of training and preparing them to exercise their powers and perform their duties under this Act after the issue of the writ.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum number of election officers appointed
Subsection.Text: Before the eighth day after the issue of the writ, a returning officer shall not appoint more than half the number of election officers referred to in subsection (1).

As soon as possible after the issue of the writ, a returning officer shall solicit names of persons suitable to be election officers from the candidates of registered parties that endorsed candidates in the last election in the electoral district or from the registered associations of those registered parties — or, if such a registered party has no registered association in that electoral district, from that registered party.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Persons whose names are provided to be appointed
Subsection.Text: If the number of names provided under subsection (1) within seven days after the issue of the writ is less than, or equal to, the number of election officers remaining to be appointed under subsection 32(1) after the returning officer has appointed election officers under section 32, the returning officer shall, subject to subsection 37(1), appoint the persons named as election officers.

If the number of names provided under subsection (1) within seven days after the issue of the writ is more than the number of election officers remaining to be appointed under subsection 32(1) after the returning officer has appointed election officers under section 32, the returning officer shall, subject to subsection 37(1), appoint from those names the election officers remaining to be appointed as far as possible in the proportion of the votes received in the last election in the electoral district by the candidates of the registered parties whose candidates or registered associations provided names under subsection (1), or that themselves provided names under that subsection, as the case may be.

If a returning officer removes or replaces an election officer, the former election officer shall return all election documents and election materials in his or her possession to the returning officer or to an authorized person.

Each returning officer shall make available to each candidate, on completion of the list, a list of the election officers for the electoral district who will be responsible for assisting the returning officer or assistant returning officer in the revision of lists of electors under Part 7.

Each election officer shall, while exercising his or her powers or performing his or her duties, wear or carry the identification that is supplied by the Chief Electoral Officer and shall show it on request.

A returning officer may, on reasonable grounds, refuse to appoint as an election officer a person recommended by a candidate or by a registered association or registered party and shall immediately advise the candidate, association or party of the refusal.

If as a result of the refusal a position is not filled and there are no more persons whose names were provided by the candidate, registered association or registered party under subsection 33(1) who may be appointed under subsection 33(2) or (3), the candidate, association or party may recommend another person within 24 hours after being advised of the refusal.

Section.MarginalNote: Record of election officers’ powers and duties
Section.Text: A returning officer shall keep a record of the powers and duties that he or she has assigned to each election officer, and of the time at which or during which each election officer is to exercise a power or perform a duty assigned to him or her.
An election officer shall exercise or perform, in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions, any power or duty assigned to him or her by a returning officer.
Subsections 41(2) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: When the Chief Electoral Officer has determined which candidates, registered associations or registered parties have the right to provide lists of names under subsection (1), he or she shall notify those registered parties without delay of that right.

Section.Text: Section 42 of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection 33(3) and section 41, in a case in which a registered party that endorsed a candidate in the last election merges with one or more parties that were registered parties at the last election,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the merged party is deemed to have endorsed a candidate in the last election in the electoral district; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that candidate is deemed to have received the same number of votes as the candidate who, among the candidates of the merging parties, received the largest number of votes at that election.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 43(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly obstruct an election officer in the exercise of his or her powers or the performance of his or her duties;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: without authority, use identification simulating that used by an election officer or intended to replace that prescribed by the Chief Electoral Officer for that purpose; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: having been removed or replaced as an election officer, fail to return any election documents or election materials in his or her possession to the returning officer or to an authorized person.

Section.Text: Subsection 43.1(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No person who is in control of an apartment building, condominium building or other multiple-residence building or a gated community shall prevent an election officer or a member of the staff of a returning officer from obtaining access to the building or gated community, as the case may be, between 9:00 a.m. and 9:00 p.m., to exercise his or her powers or perform his or her duties under this Act.

Section.Text: The heading of Part 4 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Register of Electors and Register of Future Electors

Section.Text: The heading “Maintenance and Communication of Register” before section 44 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Section 44 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a register of persons who are qualified as electors, to be known as the Register of Electors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a register of persons who are qualified as future electors, to be known as the Register of Future Electors.

Subsection.Text: The Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors shall contain, for each elector or future elector who is included in it, as the case may be, his or her surname, given names, gender, date of birth, civic address, mailing address and any other information that is provided under subsections 49(2), 211.2(4), 223(2), 233(2) and 251(3).

The Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors shall also contain, for each elector or future elector, as the case may be, a unique, randomly generated identifier that is assigned by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subject to subsection (5), the Register of Future Electors may continue to contain information about a former future elector after he or she becomes an elector until he or she is included in the Register of Electors.
Inclusion in the Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors is optional.
A future elector’s inclusion in the Register of Future Electors does not require the consent of the future elector’s parents or guardian or tutor.

Section.Text: Subsection 45(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Lists to members and registered parties

Subsection.Text: By November 15 in each year, the Chief Electoral Officer shall make available to the member for each electoral district and, on request, to each registered party that endorsed a candidate in the electoral district in the last election, a copy — taken from the Register of Electors — in electronic form, or in formats that include electronic form, of the lists of electors for the electoral district.

Section.Text: The heading before section 46 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 46(1) of the Act before subparagraph (b)(i) is replaced by the following:
The Register of Electors and the Register of Future Electors shall be updated from

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that electors or future electors, as the case may be, have given the Chief Electoral Officer, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: that is held by a federal department or body and that electors or future electors, as the case may be, have expressly authorized to be given to the Chief Electoral Officer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers reliable and necessary for updating the surname, given names, gender, date of birth, civic address and mailing address of electors and future electors included in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, as the case may be, and that

Subsection.Text: Subsection 46(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Source of information: Register of Future Electors
Subsection.Text: The Register of Electors shall be updated from information that the Chief Electoral Officer holds in the Register of Future Electors about future electors who become electors.

The Chief Electoral Officer may retain information collected under paragraph (1)(b), but not included in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, for the purpose of correlating information subsequently collected with information already contained in the relevant register.

Section.Text: Sections 46.1 and 46.2 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Information from Minister of Citizenship and Immigration
Section.Text: Despite subparagraph 46(1)(a)(ii), for the purpose of assisting the Chief Electoral Officer in updating the Register of Electors, including by deleting the name of a person who is not an elector, the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration may, at the request of the Chief Electoral Officer in writing, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with any of the following information, in relation to a person, that is contained in databases maintained by the Department of Citizenship and Immigration that relate to permanent residents and foreign nationals as those terms are defined in subsection 2(1) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act and with the date when the information referred to in paragraph (d) was included or updated in those databases:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: his or her surname and given names;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: his or her gender;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: his or her date of birth;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: his or her addresses; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any unique identifier that that Minister has assigned to him or her under that Act.

Section.Text: For the purpose of assisting the Chief Electoral Officer in updating the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, the Minister of National Revenue may, on a return of income referred to in subsection 150(1) of the Income Tax Act, request that an individual who is filing a return of income under paragraph 150(1)(d) of that Act indicate in the return whether he or she is a Canadian citizen.

Section.MarginalNote: Information in respect of deceased individuals
Section.Text: For the purpose of updating the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, the Minister of National Revenue shall, at the request of the Chief Electoral Officer, provide the name, date of birth and address of any individual to whom paragraph 150(1)(b) of the Income Tax Act applies if that individual has, in his or her last return of income filed under paragraph 150(1)(d) of that Act, authorized that Minister to provide his or her name, date of birth and address to the Chief Electoral Officer for the relevant register.
Sections 47.1 to 51 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Between election periods, a returning officer shall perform any duties related to the updating of the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors that are requested by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall, before including an elector in the Register of Electors or a future elector in the Register of Future Electors, send him or her the Chief Electoral Officer’s information relating to him or her and ask if he or she wishes to be included in the relevant register.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Obligation of elector or future elector
Subsection.Text: An elector or future elector who wishes to be included in the relevant register shall confirm, correct or complete the information, in writing, and give it to the Chief Electoral Officer along with a signed certification that he or she is qualified as an elector or as a future elector, as the case may be.
This section does not apply in respect of the inclusion of an elector or future elector that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: done at his or her request;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: done using information from lists of electors or future electors established under provincial law that contain information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers sufficient for the elector’s or future elector’s inclusion; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: done using information to update the Register of Electors under subsection 46(1.01).

Subsection.Text: Any person may at any time request the Chief Electoral Officer to include him or her in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, by providing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a signed certification that he or she is qualified as an elector or as a future elector, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: his or her surname, given names, gender, date of birth, civic address and mailing address; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: satisfactory proof of identity.

Subsection.Text: In addition to the information referred to in subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer may invite the elector or future elector to give any other information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to implement any agreements entered into under section 55, but the elector or future elector is not required to do so.

Section.Text: An elector or future elector may inform the Chief Electoral Officer of changes to the information in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, as the case may be, relating to him or her, and the Chief Electoral Officer shall make the necessary corrections to the relevant register.
The Chief Electoral Officer may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contact an elector or future elector to verify the Chief Electoral Officer’s information relating to him or her; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: request the elector or future elector to confirm, correct or complete the information within 60 days after receiving the request.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 52(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer shall delete from the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors the name of any person who
Paragraph 52(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not an elector or future elector, as the case may be, subject to subsection 44(4);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 52(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is, by reason of mental incapacity, under a court-ordered protective regime, including guardianship, tutorship or curatorship, and whose authorized representative under the regime requests in writing that the person’s name be deleted; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is a future elector with a mental incapacity whose parent requests in writing that the future elector’s name be deleted.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 52(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chief Electoral Officer may delete from the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors the name of any person who fails to comply with a request referred to in paragraph 51(b) within the 60 days.

Section.Text: Section 53 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 53(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: If a future elector so requests the Chief Electoral Officer in writing, information in the Register of Future Electors relating to the future elector shall be used only for the purposes of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: updating the Register of Electors; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of information in the course of public education and information programs implemented under subsection 18(1).

Section.Text: Section 54 of the Act is replaced by the following:
At the written request of an elector or future elector, the Chief Electoral Officer shall send him or her all the information in the Chief Electoral Officer’s possession relating to him or her.
Subsection 55(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may enter into an agreement with any body responsible under provincial law for establishing a list of electors or future electors, governing the giving of information contained in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, or the giving of information referred to in subsection 44(2) or (3) that the Chief Electoral Officer intends to include in either register, if that information is needed for establishing such a list.

Despite subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer shall not enter into an agreement governing the giving of information contained in the Register of Future Electors, or the giving of information referred to in subsection 44(2) or (3) in relation to future electors, with any body that is required under provincial law to give or to make available to political parties, entities associated with a political party or members of a legislature information relating to future electors.
Paragraphs 56(a) to (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly make a false or misleading statement, orally or in writing, relating to their qualification as an elector or as a future elector or relating to any other information referred to in section 49;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly make a false or misleading statement, orally or in writing, relating to another person’s qualification as an elector or as a future elector, to that other person’s surname, given names, gender, civic address or mailing address, or to the identifier assigned to that other person by the Chief Electoral Officer, for the purpose of having that other person’s name deleted from the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: request the listing in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors of the name of a person who is not qualified as an elector or as a future elector, as the case may be, knowing that the person is not so qualified;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly apply to have included in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors the name of an animal or thing;

Subsection.Text: Subaragraph 56(e)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of the administration and enforcement of this Act or the Referendum Act, or

Subsection.Text: Section 56 of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (e)(iii) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly use personal information that is obtained from the Register of Future Electors except as follows:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of updating the Register of Electors,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of the transmission of information in the course of public education and information programs implemented under subsection 18(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of the administration and enforcement of this Act or the Referendum Act, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: in accordance with the conditions included in an agreement made under section 55, in the case of information that is transmitted in accordance with the agreement; or

Section.Text: Paragraph 57(1.2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: fix the date for voting at the election, which date must be no earlier than the 36th day and no later than the 50th day after the day on which the writ was issued.

Section.Text: Subsections 59(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the Governor in Council orders the withdrawal of a writ, the Chief Electoral Officer shall publish a notice of the withdrawal in the Canada Gazette and, on being ordered by the Governor in Council to do so, shall, within three months after the date of publication of the notice, issue a new writ ordering an election to be held.
The day named in the new writ for polling day is determined by the Governor in Council, but may be no later than the 50th day after the day on which the new writ was issued.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Postponement of election and new polling day
Subsection.Text: If the Chief Electoral Officer certifies that by reason of a flood, fire or other disaster it is impracticable to carry out the provisions of this Act with regard to an electoral district, but the Governor in Council is of the opinion that the withdrawal of the writ under subsection (1) is not warranted, the Governor in Council may, by order, postpone the election by up to seven days for that electoral district and, correspondingly, extend the election period and fix the date for the new polling day.

If the Governor in Council orders the postponement of an election for an electoral district under subsection (4), then, in respect of that electoral district, the following rules apply in respect of any time period before polling day specified under this Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act authorizes or requires anything to be done on a day that is a certain number of days before polling day or done within a period that ends before polling day, and the day occurs or the period ends before the day on which the postponement order is made, then polling day is deemed to still be the day fixed under paragraph 57(1.2)(c) and not the day fixed under subsection (4);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act authorizes or requires anything to be done within a period that ends on or before polling day and the period ends on or after the day on which the postponement order is made, then

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: anything done as authorized or required on or before the day on which the postponement order is made is valid, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of anything not done as authorized or required on or before that day, the period during which the thing is authorized or required to be done is extended by the number of days by which the election is postponed;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if this Act authorizes or requires anything to be done on a day that is a certain number of days before polling day and that day occurs on the day on which the postponement order is made, then

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: anything done as authorized or required on that day is valid, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in respect of anything not done as authorized or required on that day, the day on which the thing is authorized or required to be done is postponed by the number of days by which the election is postponed;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if a period is extended under subparagraph (b)(ii) or a day is postponed under subparagraph (c)(ii), then any mention of a number of days before polling day prevails over any mention of a day of the week before polling day; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of this subsection, if the original polling day fixed under paragraph 57(1.2)(c) is a Tuesday because of section 56.2 or subsection 57(4), it is deemed to be a Monday.

Section.Text: Subsection 60(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Returning officer to open and maintain office

Subsection.Text: Every returning officer shall, without delay after receiving the writ or notice by the Chief Electoral Officer of the issue of the writ, open an office in premises that are accessible to electors with a disability in a convenient place in the electoral district and shall maintain the office throughout the election period.

Section.Text: Paragraph 61(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make a solemn declaration in the prescribed form; and

Section.Text: Subsection 64(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The returning officer shall make the notice of grant of a poll available to, for each polling station, an election officer who is assigned to work in the polling station. The election officer shall post the notice in the polling station.
Paragraph 65(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: les personnes qui n’ont pas qualité d’électeur le jour où leur acte de candidature est déposé;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 65(d) and (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Electoral Officer;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 66(1)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a solemn declaration, in the prescribed form, made by the prospective candidate of

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(a) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (i):

Subparagraph.Label: (i.1)
Subparagraph.Text: any other name by which he or she is commonly known — other than a name that could be confused with the name of a political party — and that he or she wishes to appear on the ballot instead of the name referred to in subparagraph (i),

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 66(1)(a)(iv) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the auditor’s name, address and occupation, if the prospective candidate has appointed an auditor under subsection 477.1(2);

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 66(1)(a)(v) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: le nom du parti politique qui la soutient ou, faute de soutien, son intention d’être désignée par la mention « indépendant(e) » ou de n’avoir aucune désignation d’appartenance politique dans les documents électoraux;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 66(1)(a) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of subparagraph (v) and by adding the following after subparagraph (v):

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: if the prospective candidate’s statement includes the name of a political party that has endorsed him or her but the returning officer is unable to verify, under paragraph 71(2)(c), that the party has done so, the prospective candidate’s choice of either one of the two options referred to in subparagraph (v) or the withdrawal of his or her nomination paper;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 66(1)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement signed by the prospective candidate consenting to the nomination;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (f), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (g) and by adding the following after paragraph (g):

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if applicable, a signed statement by the prospective candidate setting out the name of the person who is authorized by the prospective candidate under subsection 67(7).

Subsection.Text: Subsections 66(2) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:
For the purpose of subparagraphs (1)(a)(i) and (i.1), the name shall not include any title, degree or other prefix or suffix.
For the purpose of subparagraph (1)(a)(i), the occupation shall be stated briefly and shall correspond to the occupation by which the prospective candidate is known.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Witness ensures signatures are local electors’
Subsection.Text: A witness to a signature referred to in paragraph (1)(e) or (f) shall use due diligence to ensure that the signatures that are made in his or her presence are all made by electors resident in the electoral district.

Section.Text: Section 67 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A prospective candidate shall file the nomination paper with the returning officer in the electoral district in which the prospective candidate is seeking nomination at any time between the date of the issue of the Notice of Election and the close of nominations.
A prospective candidate who personally files his or her nomination paper shall provide the returning officer with the following proof of his or her identity:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one piece of identification issued by a Canadian government, whether federal, provincial or local, or an agency of such a government, that contains a photograph of the prospective candidate and his or her name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: two pieces of identification of a type authorized under subsection (3), each of which establishes that name.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may authorize types of identification for the purposes of subsections (2) and (8). For greater certainty, any document may be authorized, regardless of who issued it.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Name prospective candidate commonly known by — documents
Subsection.Text: If a prospective candidate wishes a name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i.1) to appear on the ballot instead of the name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i), he or she shall file with the returning officer documents of a type authorized under subsection (5) as proof that he or she is commonly known by that name.
The Chief Electoral Officer may authorize types of documents for the purposes of subsection (4).

If a prospective candidate has appointed an auditor, the prospective candidate shall file with the returning officer by the close of nominations a statement signed by the auditor consenting to act in that capacity.
A prospective candidate may authorize another person to carry out, on the prospective candidate’s behalf, any of the prospective candidate’s obligations under subsections (1), (4) and (6).

A person authorized under subsection (7) who files a nomination paper on behalf of a prospective candidate shall file on the prospective candidate’s behalf with the returning officer, together with the nomination paper, the following proof of the prospective candidate’s identity:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a copy, signed by the prospective candidate, of one piece of identification issued by a Canadian government, whether federal, provincial or local, or an agency of such a government, that contains a photograph of the prospective candidate and his or her name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a copy, signed by the prospective candidate, of each of two pieces of identification of a type authorized under subsection (3), each of which establishes that name.

Section.Text: Section 68 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The chief agent of a political party shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer, no later than 24 hours before the close of nominations, with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the name of every prospective candidate who has been endorsed by the party;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the electoral district in which the prospective candidate intends to be a candidate; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the person or persons who endorsed the prospective candidate on the party’s behalf.

Subsection.Text: As soon as feasible after receiving the information set out in paragraphs (3)(a) to (c), but no later than the close of nominations, the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide the information to the returning officer of the electoral district referred to in paragraph (3)(b).
Subsection 70(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Un acte de candidature ne peut être reçu de quiconque entre au bureau du directeur du scrutin après 14 h le jour de clôture.
Subsection 70(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The returning officer may authorize a person to receive — or examine, in the case of paragraph (b) — the following in any place designated by the returning officer:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nomination paper;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraphs 67(2)(a) and (b);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the documents referred to in subsection 67(4);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the statement referred to in subsection 67(6); and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the copy or copies of the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraphs 67(8)(a) and (b).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 71(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the prospective candidate’s identity has been proven by the piece or pieces of identification provided under subsection 67(2) or the copy or copies filed under subsection 67(8), as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: that the nomination paper is complete, including having at least the number of signatures referred to in paragraph 66(1)(e) or (f), as the case may be;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 71(2) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that the political party named in the nomination paper as having endorsed the prospective candidate has done so, as evidenced by the information provided under paragraph 68(3)(a) in respect of that prospective candidate.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 71:

Section.MarginalNote: Name prospective candidate commonly known by — determination

Subsection.Text: If a nomination paper includes a name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i.1) and documents have been filed under subsection 67(4) in respect of the prospective candidate, then the returning officer shall determine, in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions, whether the documents prove that the prospective candidate is commonly known by that name.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Confusion with name of political party — determination
Subsection.Text: If the returning officer determines that the documents prove that the prospective candidate is commonly known by that name, but in the returning officer’s opinion the name could be confused with the name of a political party, he or she shall notify the Chief Electoral Officer, who shall determine whether the name could be confused with one and shall inform the returning officer of the determination.
The name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i.1) shall be the name that appears on the ballot in respect of the prospective candidate unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the returning officer determines under subsection (1) that the documents filed under subsection 67(4) do not prove that the prospective candidate is commonly known by that name; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Electoral Officer determines under subsection (2) that the name could be confused with the name of a political party.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, if the name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i.1) is not, under subsection (3), the name that is to appear on the ballot in respect of the prospective candidate, then the name that is to appear on the ballot in respect of the prospective candidate shall be the name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of name to appear on ballot
Subsection.Text: The returning officer shall, not later than 48 hours after the nomination paper is filed, give the prospective candidate notice, in the prescribed form, of whether the name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i.1) is the name that is to appear on the ballot in respect of the prospective candidate.

Section.Text: Section 72 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 73(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who files a nomination paper or any other document under section 67 may do so by electronic means. In order for the nomination to be valid, the returning officer must receive the document in electronic form by the close of nominations.

If a prospective candidate files the nomination paper under section 67 by electronic means, he or she may, to prove his or her identity, file by electronic means the copy or copies referred to in paragraph 67(8)(a) or (b). If the prospective candidate does so, he or she need not provide proof of his or her identity under subsection 67(2).

Section.Text: Sections 91 and 92 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Publishing false statement to affect election results

Subsection.Text: No person or entity shall, with the intention of affecting the results of an election, make or publish, during the election period,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a false statement that a candidate, a prospective candidate, the leader of a political party or a public figure associated with a political party has committed an offence under an Act of Parliament or a regulation made under such an Act — or under an Act of the legislature of a province or a regulation made under such an Act — or has been charged with or is under investigation for such an offence; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a false statement about the citizenship, place of birth, education, professional qualifications or membership in a group or association of a candidate, a prospective candidate, the leader of a political party or a public figure associated with a political party.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies regardless of the place where the election is held or the place where the false statement is made or published.

Section.MarginalNote: Publishing false statement of candidate’s withdrawal
Section.Text: No person or entity shall publish a false statement that indicates that a candidate has withdrawn.
Subsections 93(1) and (1.1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Preliminary lists and information to returning officer

Subsection.Text: As soon as possible after the issue of a writ, the Chief Electoral Officer shall prepare a preliminary list of electors for each polling division in an electoral district, and shall make it available to the returning officer for the electoral district along with all the other information in the Register of Electors that relates to the electors of that electoral district.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall make available in electronic form or in formats that include electronic form, to each registered party or eligible party that requests them, the preliminary lists of electors for an electoral district in respect of which a writ has been issued.

Section.Text: Section 94 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Each returning officer to whom the preliminary lists of electors for his or her electoral district have been made available shall make them available to each candidate in the electoral district who requests them.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 95(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are incarcerated electors as defined in section 177;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 95(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the elector’s polling station, and whether or not it is in premises that are accessible to electors with a disability;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 95(3)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: requires a polling station that is in premises that are accessible to electors with a disability, which his or her polling station is not; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is unable to attend at a polling station because of a disability.

Section.Text: Sections 97 and 98 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Election officers who are designated to receive applications

Subsection.Text: The returning officer, assistant returning officer and other election officers for an electoral district may receive applications for additions or corrections to, or deletions from, a preliminary list of electors or the Register of Electors for their electoral district.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Applications for addition, correction or deletion
Subsection.Text: All applications referred to in subsection (1) that are received by election officers other than the returning officer or assistant returning officer shall, on completion, be presented to the returning officer or assistant returning officer for his or her approval.

Section.Text: The returning officer may open one or more offices for the revision of the preliminary lists of electors. The office or offices shall be in premises that are accessible to electors with a disability.
Section 100 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Election officers to act in pairs

Subsection.Text: Election officers shall act in pairs when they assist the returning officer or assistant returning officer in the revision of the preliminary lists of electors.

In case of disagreement between members of a pair, the two election officers shall refer the matter to the returning officer or assistant returning officer for a decision and are bound by that decision.

Section.Text: Paragraph 101(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the elector, or another elector who lives at the same residence as the elector, completes the prescribed registration form, and makes the solemn declaration in the prescribed form, at their residence and in the presence of a pair of election officers assisting the returning officer or assistant returning officer in the revision of the preliminary lists of electors.

Section.Text: The heading before section 103 and sections 103 and 104 of the Act are repealed.
Section 104.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
