Each returning officer shall, on the 19th day before polling day, make available the most current preliminary lists of electors for his or her electoral district to each candidate in the electoral district who requests them.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall, on the 19th day before polling day, make available in electronic form or in formats that include electronic form the most current preliminary lists of electors for each electoral district to each registered party that has endorsed a candidate in that electoral district and that requests them.
Sections 106 and 107 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Each returning officer shall, without delay after the 7th day before polling day but no later than the 3rd day before polling day, prepare the official list of electors for each polling station for use on polling day.

Subsection.Text: The revised list of electors for each polling division and official list of electors for each polling station shall be in the form established by the Chief Electoral Officer. The official list of electors shall indicate each elector’s polling division number.

Each returning officer shall make available to election officers the revised list of electors or official list of electors, as the case may be, that the election officers need to conduct the vote in the advance polling station or polling station to which they are assigned. The list shall indicate each elector’s year of birth.
Each returning officer shall make available to each candidate a version of the revised lists of electors and the official lists of electors that does not indicate an elector’s year of birth.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall make available in electronic form or in formats that include electronic form, for each electoral district, a version of the revised lists of electors and the official lists of electors that does not indicate an elector’s year of birth to each registered party that has endorsed a candidate in that electoral district.

Section.Text: Subsection 108(2) of the Act is repealed.
Subsections 109(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall make available in electronic form, or in formats that include electronic form, the final lists of electors for each electoral district to each registered party that has endorsed a candidate in the electoral district and to the member who was elected for the electoral district in the last election.

Section.Text: Section 110 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A registered party to which lists of electors are made available under section 45, subsection 93(1.1), section 104.2, subsection 107(4) or section 109 may use the lists for communicating with electors, including using them for soliciting contributions and recruiting party members.

An eligible party to which preliminary lists of electors are made available under subsection 93(1.1) may use the lists for communicating with electors, including using them for soliciting contributions and recruiting party members.
A member to whom lists of electors or final lists of electors are made available under section 45 or 109, respectively, may use the lists for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: communicating with his or her electors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the member is a member of a registered party, soliciting contributions for the registered party and recruiting party members.

Subsection.Text: A candidate to whom the preliminary lists of electors are made available under section 94 or 104.1, or to whom the revised lists of electors or the official lists of electors are made available under subsection 107(3), may use the lists for communicating with his or her electors during the election period, including using them for soliciting contributions and campaigning.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 111(f)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for the purposes of the administration and enforcement of this Act or the Referendum Act.

Section.Text: The heading before section 112 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsection 112(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: List of election officers to candidates

Subsection.Text: A returning officer shall, at least three days before polling day, post in his or her office, and make available to each candidate or candidate’s representative, a list of the names of all the election officers appointed to act in the electoral district who are assigned to a polling station, along with the polling station to which each officer is assigned.

Section.Text: Section 113 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chief Electoral Officer, at any time before the issue of the writ or immediately after the issue of it, shall deliver to the returning officer sufficient quantities of election materials and the necessary instructions for the election officers to exercise their powers and perform their duties.
Subsection 114(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Each ballot box shall be of the size and shape and made of the material determined by the Chief Electoral Officer and be constructed to allow seals for the use of the returning officers and other election officers to be affixed.

Section.Text: Subsection 116(6) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Les bulletins de vote doivent porter le nom de l’imprimeur qui doit, lorsqu’il les livre au directeur du scrutin, lui remettre un affidavit, selon le formulaire prescrit, précisant leur description, le nombre qu’il lui livre et le fait qu’il s’est conformé au paragraphe (5).
Subsection 117(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Ballots shall contain the names of candidates referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i) or (i.1), as the case may be, and taken from their nomination papers, arranged alphabetically.
Paragraph 117(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the information referred to in subsection 68(3) was provided in respect of the candidate in accordance with that subsection; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 117(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le bulletin de vote porte la mention « indépendant(e) » sous le nom du candidat qui l’a demandé conformément au sous-alinéa 66(1)a)(v), et seulement dans ce cas.
Subsection 117(5) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Mention de l’adresse ou de la profession
Subsection.Text: Dans les cas où au moins deux candidats ont le même nom et ont indiqué leur intention d’être désignés par la mention « indépendant(e) » ou de n’avoir aucune désignation de parti dans le cadre du sous-alinéa 66(1)a)(v), les bulletins de vote mentionnent l’adresse ou la profession de ces candidats s’ils en font la demande par écrit au directeur du scrutin avant 17 h le jour de clôture.

Section.Text: The heading before section 119 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Provision of Election Materials to Election Officers

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 119(1) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Materials to be provided to election officers

Subsection.Text: Before voting begins, each returning officer shall provide, in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions, the election officers who are assigned to a polling station in the returning officer’s electoral district with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: enough ballots for at least the number of electors on the official list of electors for the polling station;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement showing the number of ballots that are provided, with their serial numbers;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 119(1)(f) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: f)
Paragraph.Text: la liste électorale officielle à utiliser au bureau de scrutin, qu’il place si possible dans l’urne avec les bulletins de vote et autres accessoires;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 119(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Until the opening of the poll, the election officers are responsible for all election materials in their possession and shall take every precaution to ensure the safekeeping of those materials and to prevent any person from having unlawful access to them.

Section.Text: Section 120 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:
Each returning officer shall, for polling day, establish polling stations and assign each polling division to a polling station.
Subsections 121(1) to (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A polling station shall be in premises that are accessible to electors with a disability.

If a returning officer is unable to secure suitable premises for a polling station that are accessible to electors with a disability, the returning officer may, with the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer, establish the polling station in premises that are not accessible to such electors.

Each polling station shall contain a sufficient number of voting compartments arranged so that each elector is screened from observation and may, without interference or interruption, mark their ballot.

Section.Text: Subsections 122(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Polling station in school or other public building
Subsection.Text: Whenever possible, a returning officer shall establish a polling station in a school or other suitable public building.

Section.Text: Sections 123 and 124 of the Act are repealed.
Section 125.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The returning officer for an electoral district and the Chief Electoral Officer shall make the addresses of all of the polling stations in the electoral district available to, respectively, each candidate in the electoral district and each political party that has endorsed a candidate in the electoral district. That information shall be made available on the later of the 24th day before polling day and the day on which the candidate’s nomination is confirmed, along with maps of the electoral district indicating the boundaries of each polling division and the location of each polling station.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notification of change — on or before 5th day before polling day
Subsection.Text: If, on or before the 5th day before polling day, there is a change in the address of any polling station in the electoral district, the returning officer and the Chief Electoral Officer shall without delay make that information available to the candidates and the political parties respectively, along with maps of the electoral district indicating the new location of the polling station.

The maps that the Chief Electoral Officer is to make available to political parties under subsections (1) and (2) shall be made available in electronic form or in formats that include electronic form.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notification of change — after 5th day before polling day
Subsection.Text: If, after the 5th day before polling day, there is a change in the address of any polling station in the electoral district, the returning officer and the Chief Electoral Officer shall without delay make that information available to the candidates and the political parties respectively.
Paragraphs 135(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any field liaison officer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the returning officer, his or her representatives and any member of his or her staff whom he or she authorizes to be present;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: any election officer whom the returning officer authorizes to be present;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 135(1)(g) and (h) of the Act are replacing by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: if a polling division of an electoral district in which a registered party’s leader is a candidate is assigned to the polling station, any media representative who — subject to any conditions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to protect the integ­rity of the vote and the privacy of any person present at the polling station — is authorized in writing by the Chief Electoral Officer to be present and take any photograph or make any audio or video recording of the candidates as they cast their votes; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the auditor referred to in section 164.1.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 135(2) to (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

When a representative of a candidate is admitted to a polling station, the representative shall deliver his or her written authorization from the candidate or the candidate’s official agent in the prescribed form, or a copy of it, to an election officer who is assigned to the polling station.

A representative bearing a written authorization referred to in subsection (2), or a copy of one, is a representative of the candidate for the purposes of this Act and is entitled to represent the candidate to the exclusion of any elector who might otherwise claim the right to represent the candidate.
Each representative of a candidate or each elector described in paragraph (1)(d), on being admitted to the polling station, shall make a solemn declaration in the prescribed form.

A candidate’s representative who is appointed to more than one polling station shall, before being admitted to the first polling station, make a solemn declaration in the prescribed form before an election officer who is assigned to that polling station. The representative is not required to make another solemn declaration on being admitted to any other polling station in the same electoral district if he or she presents a document, in the prescribed form, proving that he or she has already made the solemn declaration.

Section.Text: Subsection 136(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Representatives may absent themselves from poll
Subsection.Text: A representative of a candidate, or an elector described in paragraph 135(1)(d), may leave a polling station at any time and return at any time before the counting of the votes begins and is not required to produce a new written authorization from the candidate or official agent, or a copy of one, or to make another solemn declaration.
Subsection 138(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Before a polling station opens on polling day, and in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present at the polling station, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall initial the back of every ballot in the space indicated in Form 3 of Schedule 1, entirely in ink or entirely in black pencil so that when the ballot is folded the initials can be seen. The initials shall be as similar as possible on each ballot.
Subsections 138(2) and (3) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:
Le fonctionnaire électoral appose son paraphe sans détacher le bulletin de vote du carnet.

L’apposition du paraphe ne peut avoir pour effet de retarder l’ouverture du scrutin; s’il n’a pas paraphé tous les bulletins de vote à l’heure d’ouverture, le fonctionnaire électoral le fait dans les meilleurs délais, avant de remettre les bulletins aux électeurs.

Section.Text: The portion of section 140 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

When the polling station opens, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, open the ballot box and ascertain that it is empty, and shall
Sections 141 and 142 of the Act are replaced by the following:
Immediately after the ballot box is sealed, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall call on the electors to vote.

An election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall ensure that every elector is admitted into the polling station and that the electors are not disturbed when they are in or near the polling station.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 143(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Elector to give name and address

Subsection.Text: Each elector, in order to receive a ballot, shall give his or her name and address to an election officer who is assigned to the polling station, and, on request, to a candidate or a candidate’s representative.
The portion of subsection 143(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If the election officer determines that the elector’s name and address appear on the list of electors or that the elector is allowed to vote under section 146, 147, 148 or 149, then, subject to subsection (3), the elector shall provide the election officer with the following proof of his or her identity and residence:
Paragraph 143(2)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one piece of identification issued by a Canadian government, whether federal, provincial or local, or an agency of such a government, that contains a photograph of the elector and his or her name and address; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 143(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer may authorize types of identification for the purposes of paragraph (2)(b). For greater certainty, any document may be authorized, regardless of who issued it.
Subsection 143(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An elector may instead prove his or her identity and residence by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing if he or she is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling station and who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provides the election officer referred to in subsection (1) with the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph (2)(a) or (b), respectively; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vouches for the elector by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(2) in writing.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vouching for electors in long-term care institution
Subsection.Text: If an elector who resides in an institution for seniors or persons with a disability wishes to prove his or her identity and residence under subsection (3), the other elector referred to in that subsection who accompanies him or her may be an employee of the institution who resides — despite that subsection — in any polling division in the elector’s electoral district or an adjacent electoral district.
In subsection (3.01), employee includes an owner of the institution and any person who occupies a management position at the institution.
Subsection 143(3.2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (3.1), if the election officer referred to in subsection (1), a candidate or a candidate’s representative has reasonable doubts concerning the residence of an elector referred to in subsection (3.1), the officer, candidate or representative may request that the elector make the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing, in which case the elector’s residence is deemed to have been proven only if he or she makes that solemn declaration.
Subsections 143(4) to (6) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the election officer is satisfied that an elector’s identity and residence have been proven in accordance with subsection (2), (3), (3.1) or (3.2), the elector’s name shall be crossed off the list and, subject to section 144, the elector shall be immediately allowed to vote.

Section.Text: Sections 143.1 and 144 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement before making solemn declaration — elector

Subsection.Text: If a person decides to prove his or her identity and residence by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing, an election officer shall, before the person makes the solemn declaration, advise him or her in writing of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who is found guilty of voting or attempting to vote at an election knowing that he or she is not qualified as an elector or who contravenes subsection 549(3).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Requirement before making solemn declaration — vouching for elector
Subsection.Text: If a person decides to vouch for an elector by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(2) in writing, an election officer shall, before the person makes the solemn declaration, advise him or her in writing of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes any of subsections 282.1(1) to (3) or 549(3).

Section.Text: If the election officer referred to in subsection 143(1), a candidate or a candidate’s representative has reasonable doubts concerning whether a person intending to vote is qualified as an elector, the officer, candidate or representative may request that the person make the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing, in which case the person shall not be allowed to vote unless he or she makes that solemn declaration.
Sections 146 to 148.1 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Name and address corresponding closely to another
Section.Text: If the name and address of a person who asks for a ballot do not appear in the list of electors but a different name and address in that list correspond so closely as to suggest that they are intended to refer to that person, the person shall not be allowed to vote unless he or she makes a solemn declaration in the prescribed form.

Section.MarginalNote: Person in whose name another has voted

Subsection.Text: If a person asks for a ballot at a polling station after someone else has voted under that person’s name, the person shall not be allowed to vote unless he or she makes the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing.

An election officer shall, before the person makes the solemn declaration, advise the person in writing of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who is found guilty of voting or attempting to vote more than once contrary to section 281.5 or of requesting or applying for a ballot or special ballot in a name that is not his or her own contrary to paragraph 281.7(1)(a).

Section.MarginalNote: Name crossed off list in error
Section.Text: If an elector claims that his or her name has been crossed off in error from an official list of electors under subsection 176(2) or (3), the elector shall not be allowed to vote unless the returning officer verifies that the elector’s name was crossed off in error or the elector makes the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing.

Section.MarginalNote: Failure to prove identity or residence

Subsection.Text: An elector who fails to prove his or her identity and residence in accordance with section 143 or to make a solemn declaration otherwise required by this Act shall not receive a ballot or be allowed to vote.

Subsection.MarginalNote: When elector refuses to make solemn declaration
Subsection.Text: If an elector refuses to make a solemn declaration on the ground that he or she is not required to do so under this Act, the elector may appeal to the returning officer. If, after consultation with the election officer in whose opinion the elector is required to make the solemn declaration, the returning officer decides that the elector is not required to make it, and if the elector is entitled to vote in the polling division, the returning officer shall direct that he or she be allowed to do so.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 149(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the elector gives the election officer referred to in subsection 143(1) a transfer certificate described in section 158 or 159 and, for a certificate described in subsection 158(2), fulfils the conditions described in subsection 158(3); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the elector gives the election officer a registration certificate described in subsection 161(4).

Section.Text: Section 150 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every elector who is admitted to vote shall be given a ballot by the election officer referred to in subsection 143(1), after that election officer has placed on the back of the ballot, in the space indicated in Form 3 of Schedule 1, the number of the elector’s polling division.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Instructions to elector on receiving ballot
Subsection.Text: The election officer shall explain to each elector how to indicate his or her choice and fold the ballot so that its serial number and the initials of the election officer who initialed the ballot are visible and shall direct the elector to return the marked and folded ballot.
Paragraph 151(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: fold the ballot as instructed by the election officer; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 151(1)(d) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: return the ballot to the election officer who provided it.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 151(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The election officer shall, on receiving the ballot from the elector,

Section.Text: Subsection 152(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the ballot is incapable of being used, the elector shall return it to the election officer referred to in subsection 143(1), who shall mark it as a spoiled ballot, place it in the envelope supplied for the purpose and give the elector another ballot.

Section.Text: Section 154 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On request by an elector who is unable to read or because of a disability is unable to vote in the manner described by this Act, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall assist the elector in the presence of another election officer who is assigned to the polling station.
An election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, on request, provide a template to an elector who has a visual impairment to assist him or her in marking his or her ballot.
Subsection 155(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Assistance by friend or related person

Subsection.Text: If an elector requires assistance to vote, one of the following persons may accompany the elector into the voting compartment and assist the elector to mark his or her ballot:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a friend of the elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the elector’s spouse or common-law partner; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a relative of the elector or of the elector’s spouse or common-law partner.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 155(2) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 155(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A person described in subsection (1) who wishes to assist an elector in marking a ballot shall first make a solemn declaration, in the prescribed form, that he or she
Paragraphs 155(3)(a) to (d) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: marquera le bulletin de vote conformément aux instructions de l’électeur;

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: ne divulguera pas le vote de l’électeur;

Paragraph.Label: c)
Paragraph.Text: ne tentera pas d’exercer une influence sur celui-ci dans son choix;

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: n’a pas déjà aidé, lors de l’élection en cours, une autre personne, à titre d’ami, à voter.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 155(4) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Section 156 of the Act is replaced by the following:

An election officer who is assigned to a polling station may appoint a language or sign language interpreter to assist election officers at the polling station in communicating to an elector any information that is necessary to enable him or her to vote.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 157(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Elector who is confined to bed

Subsection.Text: At a polling station that has been established in an institution for seniors or persons with a disability, when an election officer who is assigned to the polling station considers it necessary, the election officer shall
Paragraph 157(1)(b) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Text.Language: avec l’approbation du responsable de l’établissement, transporter l’urne, les bulletins de vote et les autres documents électoraux nécessaires de chambre en chambre, en vue de recueillir les votes des électeurs alités qui résident habituellement dans la section de vote où se trouve l’établissement.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 157(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

When the vote of an elector who is confined to bed is taken, an election officer assigned to the polling station shall give the elector the assistance necessary to enable the elector to vote, and not more than one representative of each candidate may be present.

Section.Text: Subsections 158(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An election officer who is assigned to a polling station shall issue a transfer certificate to any person — other than himself or herself — whose name appears on the official list of electors for the polling station and who has been appointed to act as an election officer for another polling station in the same electoral district.

A transfer certificate issued under subsection (2) authorizes the person to vote at the polling station named in it only if, on polling day, the person exercises the power or performs the duty specified in the certificate at the place mentioned in the certificate.

Section.Text: Section 159 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Transfer certificate for elector with disability

Subsection.Text: An elector who has a disability and is therefore unable to vote without difficulty in his or her polling station may apply for a transfer certificate to vote at another polling station in the same electoral district.
The application shall be made in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Issue of transfer certificate to elector with disability
Subsection.Text: An election officer shall issue a transfer certificate in the prescribed form, and provide the certificate to the person who submitted the application to the officer, if the officer is satisfied that the elector’s name appears on a list of electors for the electoral district.
The portion of section 160 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Signing, numbering and recording transfer certificate
Section.Text: The election officer who issues a transfer certificate shall

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 160(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if possible, send a copy of the certificate to an election officer who is assigned to the polling station on whose list of electors appears the name of the person to whom the certificate has been issued.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 161(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An elector whose name is not on the list of electors may register in person on polling day before an election officer if the elector
Paragraph 161(1)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: soit en présentant la pièce visée à l’alinéa 143(2)a) sur laquelle figure une adresse qui établit sa résidence ou les pièces visées à l’alinéa 143(2)b) dont au moins une porte une telle adresse;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 161(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: proves his or her identity and residence by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing and is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling station and who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: provides the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing either an address that proves that other elector’s residence or an address that is consistent with information related to that other elector that appears on the list of electors, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: vouches for the elector by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(2) in writing.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 161(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vouching for electors in long-term care institution
Subsection.Text: If an elector who resides in an institution for seniors or persons with a disability wishes to prove his or her identity and residence under subsection (1), the other elector referred to in paragraph (1)(b) who accompanies him or her may be an employee of the institution who resides — despite that paragraph — in any polling division in the elector’s electoral district or an adjacent electoral district.
In subsection (2), employee includes an owner of the institution and any person who occupies a management position at the institution.
The election officer shall permit each candidate, or one representative of each candidate, in the electoral district to be present when an elector is being registered.
Subsection 161(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the elector satisfies the requirements of subsection (1), the election officer shall complete a registration certificate in the prescribed form authorizing the elector to vote and the elector shall sign it. The registration certificate shall include a solemn declaration made by the elector that he or she is qualified as an elector under section 3.
Subsections 161(6) and (7) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: Section 161.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement before making solemn declaration — elector

Subsection.Text: If a person decides to prove his or her identity and residence by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing, an election officer shall, before the person makes the solemn declaration, advise him or her in writing of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 161(5.1) or 549(3).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Requirement before making solemn declaration — vouching for elector
Subsection.Text: If a person decides to vouch for an elector by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(2) in writing, an election officer shall, before the person makes the solemn declaration, advise him or her in writing of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes any of subsections 282.1(1) to (3) or 549(3).

Section.Text: The heading before section 162 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Duties of Election Officers Assigned to Polling Station

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 162 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: An election officer assigned to a polling station — and not necessarily the same election officer for all of the following — shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make, on the prescribed form, the entries required under this Act;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 162(d) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraphs 162(f) and (g) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: indicate, if applicable, on the prescribed form that the elector has made a solemn declaration and the type of solemn declaration;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: indicate, if applicable, on the prescribed form that the elector refused to comply with a legal requirement to provide the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, or to make a solemn declaration;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 162(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: indicate, if applicable, on the prescribed form that an elector has voted in the circumstances described in section 147, that the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) has been made or that any other solemn declaration that was required to be made was made, note any objection that was made on behalf of any of the candidates and indicate the candidate’s name;

Section.Text: Section 164 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Procedure in case of contravention of secrecy

Subsection.Text: It is the duty of the election officers who are present at the polling station to draw the attention of any elector to an offence that the elector commits in contravening any of paragraphs 281.6(3)(a) to (c) and to the punishment to which the elector is liable, but the elector shall be allowed to vote in the usual way if he or she has not already done so.
For greater certainty, if one election officer performs his or her duty under subsection (1) with regard to an elector, no other election officer need do so with regard to that elector.

Section.Text: Section 164.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

For each general election and by-election, the Chief Electoral Officer shall engage an auditor that he or she considers to have technical or specialized knowledge — other than a member of his or her staff or an election officer — to perform an audit and report on whether election officers have properly exercised any of the powers conferred on them under this Act, or properly performed any of the duties imposed on them under this Act, that are specified by the Chief Electoral Officer.
Subsection 166(1) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by repealing paragraph (c).
Section 167 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 168(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

When a request is made to a returning officer not later than four days after the issue of the writ, the returning officer may, with the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer, combine two advance polling districts into one district.
Subsection 168(5) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Demande de modification de l’emplacement d’un bureau de vote par anticipation
Subsection.Text: Si une demande de modification de l’emplacement d’un bureau de vote par anticipation est présentée au directeur du scrutin au plus tard quatre jours après la délivrance du bref, celui-ci peut, avec l’agrément préalable du directeur général des élections, prendre des dispositions en vue de changer le bureau de place.
Subsections 168(6) and (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:
An advance polling station shall be in premises that are accessible to electors with a disability.

If a returning officer is unable to secure suitable premises for an advance polling station that are accessible to electors with a disability, the returning officer may, with the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer, establish the advance polling station in premises that are not accessible to such electors.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Advance polling station in more than one premises
Subsection.Text: If the returning officer is of the opinion that an advance polling district consists of or includes remote, isolated or low-density communities, the returning officer may, with the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer and in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions, establish the advance polling station for that district in premises in more than one such community, and have the election officers who are assigned to the advance polling station — along with the ballot box, ballots and other necessary election documents — be at given ones of those premises on different days of advance polling to take electors’ votes. For greater certainty, subsections (5) to (7) apply to the advance polling station.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 168:

Subsection.Text: A candidate whose name appears on the revised list of electors for an advance polling station is entitled on request to receive a transfer certificate to vote at another advance polling station in the same electoral district.

An election officer who is assigned to an advance polling station shall issue a transfer certificate to any person — other than himself or herself — whose name appears on the revised list of electors for the advance polling station and who has been appointed to act as an election officer for another advance polling station.

A transfer certificate issued under subsection (2) authorizes the person to vote at the advance polling station named in it only if, on one of the days of advance polling, the person performs the duty specified in the certificate at the place mentioned in the certificate.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfer certificate for elector whose advance polling station has moved
Subsection.Text: If an elector’s advance polling station moves to another location after the notice of confirmation of registration has been sent, an elector who attends at the advance polling station set out in the notice is entitled on request to receive a transfer certificate to vote at that advance polling station.

Section.MarginalNote: Transfer certificate for elector with disability

Subsection.Text: An elector who has a disability and is therefore unable to vote without difficulty in his or her advance polling station may apply for a transfer certificate to vote at another advance polling station in the same electoral district.
The application shall be made in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Issue of transfer certificate to elector with disability
Subsection.Text: An election officer shall issue a transfer certificate in the prescribed form, and provide the certificate to the person who submitted the application to the officer, if the officer is satisfied that the elector’s name appears on the revised list of electors for the electoral district.

Section.MarginalNote: Signing, numbering and recording transfer certificate
Section.Text: The election officer who issues a transfer certificate shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fill in and sign the certificate and mention on it the date of its issue;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: consecutively number the certificate in the order of its issue;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: keep a record of the certificate in the order of its issue on the prescribed form;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: not issue the certificate in blank; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: if possible, send a copy of the certificate to an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station on whose list of electors appears the name of the person to whom the certificate has been issued.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 169(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Every elector whose name is not on the revised list of electors may register in person, at the advance polling station where the elector is entitled to vote, before an election officer who is assigned to that advance polling station.
The portion of subsection 169(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
Il ne peut toutefois être inscrit que s’il établit son identité et sa résidence :

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: soit en présentant la pièce visée à l’alinéa 143(2)a) sur laquelle figure une adresse qui établit sa résidence ou les pièces visées à l’alinéa 143(2)b) dont au moins une porte une telle adresse;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 169(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: proves his or her identity and residence by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing and is accompanied by another elector whose name appears on the list of electors for the same polling station and who

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: provides the piece or pieces of identification referred to in paragraph 143(2)(a) or (b), respectively, the piece or one of those pieces containing either an address that proves that other elector’s residence or an address that is consistent with information related to that other elector that appears on the list of electors, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: vouches for the elector by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(2) in writing.

Subsection.Text: Section 169 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Vouching for electors in long-term care institution
Subsection.Text: If an elector who resides in an institution for seniors or persons with a disability wishes to prove his or her identity and residence under subsection (2), the other elector referred to in paragraph (2)(b) who accompanies him or her may be an employee of the institution who resides — despite that paragraph — in any polling division in the elector’s electoral district or an adjacent electoral district.
In subsection (2.01), employee includes an owner of the institution and any person who occupies a management position at the institution.
Subsections 169(3) and (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the elector satisfies the requirements of subsection (2), an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall complete a registration certificate in the prescribed form authorizing the elector to vote and the elector shall sign it. The registration certificate shall include a solemn declaration made by the elector that he or she is qualified as an elector under section 3.
An election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall indicate on the prescribed form the names of the electors who are permitted to vote under this section.
Subsections 169(5) and (6) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: Section 169.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Requirement before making solemn declaration — elector

Subsection.Text: If a person decides to prove his or her identity and residence by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing, an election officer shall, before the person makes the solemn declaration, advise him or her in writing of the qualifications for electors and the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes subsection 169(4.1) or 549(3).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Requirement before making solemn declaration — vouching for elector
Subsection.Text: If a person decides to vouch for an elector by making the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(2) in writing, an election officer shall, before the person makes the solemn declaration, advise him or her in writing of the penalty that may be imposed under this Act on a person who contravenes any of subsections 282.1(1) to (3) or 549(3).

Section.Text: Subsection 171(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When advance polling stations to be open
Subsection.Text: An advance polling station shall be open from 9:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m. on Friday, Saturday, Sunday and Monday, the 10th, 9th, 8th and 7th days, respectively, before polling day, and shall not be open at any other time.
Subparagraphs 172(a)(iii) and (iv) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the place where, for each advance polling station, an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall count the number of votes cast at the advance polling station, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: that the counting of the votes cast shall begin on polling day as soon after the close of the polling stations as possible or, with the Chief Electoral Officer’s prior approval, one hour before the close of the polling stations; and

Subsection.Text: Section 172 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make available to each candidate in the electoral district a map of the electoral district indicating the boundaries of each advance polling district and the location of each advance polling station.

Subsection.Text: Section 172 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 172(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maps made available to registered parties
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall, not later than Saturday, the 16th day before polling day, make available to each registered party maps of each electoral district — in electronic form or in formats that include electronic form — indicating the boundaries of each advance polling district within the electoral district and the location of each advance polling station.

Section.Text: Subsections 173(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Elector not on the revised list
Subsection.Text: An elector whose name is not on the revised list of electors may not vote at an advance polling station unless the elector has obtained a transfer certificate under section 168.1 or 168.2 or a registration certificate under subsection 169(3).

If an elector whose name does not appear on the revised list of electors has voted, an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall indicate on the prescribed form that the elector has voted in accordance with subsection (2).

Section.Text: Sections 174 and 175 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an elector whose name is on the list of electors makes a request to vote at an advance polling station that is established for his or her polling division, an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall permit the elector to vote unless the elector fails to prove his or her identity and residence in accordance with section 143 or to make a solemn declaration otherwise required by this Act.

An election officer who gives an elector a ballot at an advance polling station shall, before providing the ballot, place on the back of the ballot, in the space indicated in Form 3 of Schedule 1 for the polling division number, the number of the elector’s advance polling district.

An election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall keep a record in duplicate at the advance polling station, in the prescribed form, of the names of all persons who vote at the advance polling station, in the order in which they vote, and shall mark on the record the notations that an election officer who is assigned to a polling station is required by this Act to make opposite an elector’s name at the polling station on polling day.

At the opening of an advance polling station at 9:00 a.m., an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the 1st day of advance polling,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: open the ballot box and ascertain that it is empty,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: seal the ballot box with the seals provided by the Chief Electoral Officer, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and keep it there until the close of the advance polling station on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the 2nd, 3rd and 4th days of advance polling, place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and keep it there until the close of the advance polling station on that day.

Subsection.Text: At the close of an advance polling station at 9:00 p.m. on each of the four days of advance polling, an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, and in accordance with instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote, take the measures set out in those instructions.

If an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station determines, in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions, that another ballot box is needed at the advance polling station, then an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present and in accordance with those instructions,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: take the steps set out in subparagraphs (1)(a)(i) to (iii) with regard to that other ballot box; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take the steps set out in paragraph (1)(b) and in subsection (2) with regard to the ballot boxes in the circumstances set out in those instructions.

Subsection.Text: The candidates or their representatives may take note of the serial numbers of the seals on the following ballot boxes at the times indicated:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for any ballot box that was used on a day of advance polling, note may be taken when an advance polling station closes on each of the four days of advance polling;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for a ballot box that is placed on the table in accordance with subsection (1) or (3), note may be taken when it is placed there; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for each of the ballot boxes that were used for advance polling, note may be taken when the votes are counted on polling day.

Subsection.Text: Until the counting of the ballots on polling day, an election officer shall keep the sealed ballot box or boxes in his or her custody in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions.

Despite subsection (5), the returning officer may recover any ballot box that another election officer is required under that subsection to have in his or her custody if the returning officer considers that such action is necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote. The returning officer shall inform the Chief Electoral Officer of the matter as soon as possible.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Recovery of ballot box — dwelling place or vehicle
Subsection.Text: If a ballot box to be recovered is in a dwelling place or a vehicle, the returning officer may enter the dwelling place or vehicle without the occupant’s or owner’s consent only if the returning officer is accompanying a peace officer who enters the dwelling place or vehicle under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (8).

On ex parte application by the returning officer, a justice of the peace may issue a warrant authorizing a peace officer to enter a dwelling place or a vehicle, accompanied by the returning officer, if the justice is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a ballot box is in the dwelling place or vehicle;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry to the dwelling place or vehicle is necessary to recover the ballot-box; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: entry was refused by the occupant or owner or there are reasonable grounds to believe that entry will be refused by, or that consent to entry cannot be obtained from, the occupant or owner.

Subsection.Text: If a returning officer believes that it would not be practical to appear personally to make an application for a warrant under subsection (8), a warrant may be issued by telephone or other means of telecommunication on application submitted by telephone or other means of telecommunication, and section 487.1 of the Criminal Code applies for that purpose with any necessary modifications.

Section.Text: Subsection 176(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an official list of electors is sent to a polling station containing the names of electors that appear in the record of votes cast at an advance polling station as having already voted, the returning officer shall instruct an election officer who is assigned to the polling station to cross their names off the list, and the election officer shall do so without delay.
The portion of section 177 of the Act before the definition administrative centre is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: The definitions in this section apply in this Part, and Parts 11.1 and 19.

Subsection.Text: The definitions administrative centre, Canadian Forces elector, deputy returning officer, statement of ordinary residence and voting territory in section 177 of the Act are repealed.
The definitions application for registration and special ballot, inner envelope, liaison officer and outer envelope in section 177 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: application for registration and special ballot means an application completed by an elector in order to vote under this Part. (demande d’inscription et de bulletin de vote spécial)
inner envelope means an envelope, including one that is supplied by the Chief Electoral Officer, in which a ballot or special ballot is to be enclosed after it has been marked. (enveloppe intérieure)
liaison officer means an elector designated as a liaison officer under subsection 199.2(1) or a person appointed as a liaison officer under subsection 248(1). (agent de liaison)

outer envelope means an envelope, including one that is supplied by the Chief Electoral Officer, in which a ballot or special ballot is to be transmitted after it has been marked and enclosed in an inner envelope. (enveloppe extérieure)

Subsection.Text: The definition special ballot in section 177 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: special ballot means a ballot, other than a ballot referred to in section 241, that is provided to an elector who is entitled to vote under this Part. (bulletin de vote spécial)

Subsection.Text: Section 177 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: unit election officer means an elector designated as a unit election officer under paragraph 205(1)(b). (fonctionnaire électoral d’unité)

Section.Text: Section 180 of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 182(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: retain the solemn declaration of each special ballot officer that is made under subsection 23(1);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 182(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: obtain from the liaison officers the lists of the names of unit election officers that the commanding officers are required to provide;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 186:

Without delay after the writs are issued, the special voting rules administrator shall distribute to commanding officers and, as the special voting rules administrator considers appropriate, to any other person or to any place the election materials necessary to carry out the purposes and provisions of this Part, including materials for the purpose of determining in which electoral district an elector is entitled to vote.
Sections 188 and 189 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Without delay after a list of candidates is established, the special voting rules administrator shall provide the list to the coordinating officer, to every liaison officer and commanding officer and, as the special voting rules administrator considers appropriate, to any other person.
The heading “Definitions” before section 190 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The definition elector in section 190 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: elector means a person referred to in section 191. (électeur)

Subsection.Text: Section 190 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: alternative address means the address of the office of the returning officer for the electoral district of the elector’s place of ordinary residence. (adresse de substitution)
service number means a unique identifier assigned by the Canadian Forces under the National Defence Act to each member of the Canadian Forces. (numéro matricule)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 190:

For the purpose of section 9, subsections 23(1) and (2) and section 23.1, the coordinating officer, liaison officers designated under subsection 199.2(1), commanding officers and unit election officers are deemed to be election officers and paragraph 43(c), subsection 484(1) and paragraph 484(3)(f) also apply to them if they are relieved of duty.
The heading before section 191 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 191 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Any of the following persons is entitled to vote under this Division if he or she is qualified as an elector under section 3:

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 191(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a member of the reserve force of the Canadian Forces; and

Subsection.Text: Section 191 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by repealing paragraph (d).

Section.Text: Sections 192 to 198 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: When a person becomes entitled to vote under this Division, the person’s commanding officer shall inform him or her of the right to ask the Chief Electoral Officer to include him or her in the Register of Electors or, if already included in it, to ask for his or her registration to be updated.

When a future elector is enrolled in the Canadian Forces, or when a member of the Canadian Forces becomes qualified as a future elector, the future elector’s commanding officer shall inform him or her of the right to ask the Chief Electoral Officer to include him or her in the Register of Future Electors or, if already included in it, to ask for his or her registration to be updated.

An elector, or a future elector who is a member of the Canadian Forces, who asks the Chief Electoral Officer to include him or her in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, or asks for his or her registration to be updated, must provide his or her service number to the Chief Electoral Officer.

Section.Text: Subsection 199(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The coordinating officer shall, on request, provide to the Chief Electoral Officer the following information relating to each elector or future elector:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: his or her surname, given names, gender, rank and service number;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: his or her date of birth;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the civic address and mailing address of his or her place of ordinary residence; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: his or her current mailing address.

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may use the information provided under subsection (2) to update the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors.

The Chief Electoral Officer may retain the information provided under subsection (2), but not included in the Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors, for the purpose of correlating information subsequently collected with information already contained in the relevant register.

The Chief Electoral Officer may provide the coordinating officer with the information referred to in paragraphs (2)(a) to (d) relating to each elector or future elector who has provided a service number under this Part.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 199:
The coordinating officer shall cooperate with the special voting rules administrator to facilitate the voting, under Divisions 3 and 4 of this Part, of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person employed by or in support of the Canadian Forces outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person who accompanies a unit or other element of the Canadian Forces that is on service or active service outside Canada, within the meaning of subsection 61(1) of the National Defence Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a person who resides outside Canada with a member of the Canadian Forces or with a person referred to in paragraph (a) or (b).

Subsection.Text: The Minister of National Defence shall designate one or more electors as liaison officers to work, during and between election periods, with the Chief Electoral Officer and the coordinating officer in carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Division.
The coordinating officer shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the name and address of each liaison officer as each one is designated.

Section.Text: Sections 200 to 203 of the Act are replaced by the following:
Without delay after the issue of the writs, the Chief Electoral Officer shall inform the Minister of National Defence and the coordinating officer of their issue.
On being informed of the issue of the writs, the coordinating officer shall inform each liaison officer and commanding officer of their issue.

On being informed of the issue of the writs, a liaison officer shall give each commanding officer in respect of whose unit the liaison officer has liaison duties all necessary information for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Division.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 204(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the commanding officer will designate one or more unit election officers to collect their votes and fix the voting times during the voting period.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 204(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The list shall be arranged alphabetically and shall indicate each elector’s surname, given names and service number, the address of his or her place of ordinary residence and his or her electoral district.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 204:

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of maintaining operational security, a commanding officer may indicate on the list of electors, with respect to some or all of the electors in the commanding officer’s unit, their alternative address rather than the address that would otherwise be indicated under subsection 204(3).

For the purpose of maintaining operational security or out of a reasonable apprehension of bodily harm if the address of his or her place of ordinary residence were to appear on the list of electors, an elector may ask his or her commanding officer not to indicate that address on the list of electors. The commanding officer, unless he or she considers that it would not be in the public interest to do so, shall grant the request and indicate the elector’s alternative address on the list.
The commanding officer shall inform the special voting rules administrator, through a liaison officer, that an alternative address has been indicated under this section.
For greater certainty, the indication of an alternative address on the list of electors under this section does not change the elector’s place of ordinary residence for the purposes of this Act.
The portion of subsection 205(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
After being informed of the issue of the writs, but no later than the 28th day before polling day, each commanding officer shall
Paragraphs 205(1)(b) to (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: designate a sufficient number of electors as unit election officers to allow for voting to take place at each polling station;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: through a liaison officer, provide the special voting rules administrator with a list of the unit election officers and their ranks, and the list of electors for the unit; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide the unit election officers with the list of electors for the unit.

Section.Text: Section 207 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The commanding officers of units may establish a joint polling station for electors in their units, if the commanding officers consider that it would be useful to do so for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Division.
Paragraphs 209(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: distribute the materials to the unit election officers; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: display the list in one or more conspicuous places or make it otherwise accessible to electors in their unit.

Section.Text: Sections 210 to 212 of the Act are replaced by the following:
During the voting period, a unit election officer shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: display the voting instructions provided by the Chief Electoral Officer in relation to the vote under this Division in conspicuous places at each polling station or make them otherwise accessible to electors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: keep available for consultation by the electors the text of this Part, the election materials necessary for determining in which electoral district an elector is entitled to vote and the list of candidates.

Section.Text: A Canadian citizen may represent a registered party at a polling station if he or she provides the unit election officer with a copy of an authorization in the prescribed form signed by a candidate for the party.

Subsection.Text: Each elector, on arriving at the polling station, shall give his or her name and service number and the address of his or her place of ordinary residence to the unit election officer and, on request, to a representative of a registered party.
For the purpose of subsection (1), if an alternative address is indicated on the list of electors under section 204.1 in respect of the elector, the elector may provide that alternative address.

If the unit election officer determines that the elector’s name, service number and address appear on the list of electors, then the elector shall provide the officer with the following proof of the elector’s identity:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one piece of identification issued by the Canadian Forces that contains a photograph of the elector and his or her name and service number; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: two pieces of identification of a type authorized under section 211.3 each of which establishes the elector’s name and at least one of which establishes his or her service number.

Subsection.Text: A representative of a registered party may examine, but not handle, any piece of identification presented under this section.
If the unit election officer is satisfied that an elector’s identity has been proven in accordance with this section, the elector’s name shall be crossed off the list of electors.

Section.MarginalNote: Application for registration and special ballot

Subsection.Text: An elector whose name does not appear on the list of electors or whose address on the list does not correspond with the elector’s place of ordinary residence may complete an application for registration and special ballot and provide the unit election officer with the following proof of the elector’s identity:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: one piece of identification issued by the Canadian Forces that contains a photograph of the elector and his or her name and service number; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: two pieces of identification of a type authorized under section 211.3 each of which establishes the elector’s name and at least one of which establishes his or her service number.

Subsection.Text: An application for registration and special ballot shall be in the prescribed form and shall include the following information relating to each elector:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: his or her surname and given names;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: his or her date of birth;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: his or her service number;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the civic address and mailing address of his or her place of ordinary residence;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: his or her current mailing address; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to determine the electoral district in which he or she is entitled to vote.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of maintaining operational security or out of a reasonable apprehension of bodily harm if he or she were to indicate the addresses referred to in paragraph 2(d), the elector may apply to the elector’s commanding officer to use another address for that purpose. The commanding officer, unless he or she considers that it would not be in the public interest to do so, shall grant the application and provide the elector with the alternative address that is to be used on the application for registration and special ballot.

In addition to the information specified in subsection (2), the Chief Electoral Officer may request that the elector provide other information that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary for implementing agreements made under section 55, but the elector is not required to provide that information.
A representative of a registered party may examine, but not handle, any piece of identification presented under this section.

If the unit election officer is satisfied that an elector’s identity has been proven in accordance with this section, the unit election officer shall accept the application for registration and special ballot.
If an alternative address is used in accordance with subsection (3), subsections 204.1(3) and (4) apply with any necessary modifications.

Section.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may authorize types of identification, in consultation with the coordinating officer, for the purposes of paragraphs 211.1(3)(b) and 211.2(1)(b). For greater certainty, any document may be authorized, regardless of who issued it.

The unit election officer shall require the elector whose name has been crossed off the list of electors or whose application for registration and special ballot has been accepted to sign the declaration prescribed by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 213(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A unit election officer shall, once the elector has signed the declaration referred to in section 212, give the elector a special ballot, an inner envelope, the declaration — if it is not on the outer envelope — and the outer envelope.
An elector who has received a special ballot under subsection (1) may vote only under this Division.
The portion of subsection 213(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The elector shall use the special ballot to vote by writing on it, in private, the name of the candidate of his or her choice, folding it and, in the presence of the unit election officer,
Paragraph 213(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: placing the inner envelope and the declaration — if it is not on the outer envelope — in the outer envelope and sealing the outer envelope.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 213(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If the special ballot is incapable of being used, the elector shall return it to the unit election officer, who shall mark it as a spoiled ballot and give the elector another special ballot.
Subsection 214(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Information to be provided to elector

Subsection.Text: The unit election officer shall inform an elector that, in order for the special ballot to be counted, the outer envelope must be received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on polling day. The unit election officer shall inform the elector of the service provided by the Canadian Forces to deliver the outer envelope.
Subsection 214(3) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Section 215 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A unit election officer who is qualified to vote may vote in accordance with this Division.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 216(1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
If an elector is unable to read or because of a disability is unable to vote in the manner described in this Division, the unit election officer shall assist him or her by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: completing the declaration referred to in section 212 and writing the elector’s name where the elector’s signature is to be written; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 216(1)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: marking the special ballot as directed by the elector in the elector’s presence and in the presence of another elector selected by the elector as a witness.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 216(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
The unit election officer and an elector acting as a witness shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sign a note on the declaration indicating that the elector was assisted; and

Section.Text: Subsections 217(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Unit election officer for hospitalized electors
Subsection.Text: If no unit election officer has been designated for a service hospital or convalescent institution, the unit election officer for the unit to which the hospital or institution belongs is the unit election officer for electors who are patients in the hospital or institution.

A unit election officer for electors who are patients in a service hospital or convalescent institution may, if that officer considers it advisable and the officer in charge of the hospital or institution approves, go from room to room to administer and collect the votes of electors who are confined to bed.

Section.Text: Sections 218 and 219 of the Act are replaced by the following:

An elector who provides satisfactory evidence of his or her absence from his or her unit during the voting times fixed for the polling stations in that unit because of duty, leave or furlough may apply to a unit election officer of another unit, in accordance with section 211.2, to vote at that officer’s polling station.

Section.MarginalNote: Delivery of documents to the commanding officer

Subsection.Text: The unit election officer shall deliver to the unit’s commanding officer,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the end of each voting day, if feasible, and no later than the end of the voting period, the outer envelopes containing the marked special ballots, the applications for registration and special ballot that were accepted by the unit election officer and the service number of each elector who received a special ballot; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
