Paragraph.Text: at the end of the voting period, any spoiled outer envelopes, any spoiled special ballots and any other election documents and election materials in the unit election officer’s possession.

Subsection.Text: On receipt of the outer envelopes and applications for registration and special ballot referred to in paragraph (1)(a), the commanding officer shall deliver them to the special voting rules administrator.

On receipt of the service numbers referred to in paragraph (1)(a), the commanding officer shall inform the special voting rules administrator, through the liaison officer, of the identity of the electors who received a special ballot.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Delivery of election documents and election materials
Subsection.Text: On receipt of the election documents and election materials referred to in paragraph (1)(b), the commanding officer shall deliver them to the special voting rules administrator with all other election documents and election materials in the commanding officer’s possession.

On being informed under subsection 219(1.2) that an elector has received a special ballot, the special voting rules administrator shall inform the returning officer for the electoral district of the elector’s place of ordinary residence of this.
When the returning officer is informed under subsection (1) that an elector has received a special ballot, he or she shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the elector’s name is not already included on a list of electors, enter the elector’s name on the list of electors for the appropriate polling division in that electoral district; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: indicate on the list of electors that the elector has received a special ballot.

Section.Text: The heading of Division 3 of Part 11 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The definitions elector and register in section 220 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: elector means an elector who resides outside Canada. (électeur)
register means the register referred to in section 222. (registre)

Section.Text: Sections 221 and 222 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Inclusion in register of electors resident outside Canada
Section.Text: An elector may vote under this Division if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: his or her application for registration and special ballot is received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on the 6th day before polling day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: his or her name is entered on the register.

Section.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain a register of electors in which is entered the surname, given names, gender, date of birth, civic address, mailing address and electoral district of each elector who has filed an application for registration and special ballot in order to vote under this Division and who, at any time before making the application, resided in Canada.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 223(1)(b) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraphs 223(1)(d) to (f) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the elector’s place of ordinary residence;

Section.Text: Section 226 of the Act is amended by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (d), by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (e) and by repealing paragraph (f).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 227(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

After approving an application for registration and special ballot and after the issue of the writs, the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide a special ballot to every elector whose name is entered in the register.
Paragraph 227(2)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: placing the ballot in an inner envelope and sealing it;

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 227(2)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: signing the declaration prescribed by the Chief Electoral Officer; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: placing the inner envelope and the declaration — if it is not on the outer envelope — in the outer envelope and sealing it.

Section.Text: Section 229 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The special ballot must be received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on polling day in order to be counted.
Sections 231 and 232 of the Act are replaced by the following:
For the purpose of this Division, elector means an elector, other than an incarcerated elector, who resides in Canada and who wishes to vote in accordance with this Division.

Section.MarginalNote: Conditions for voting by special ballot

Subsection.Text: An elector may vote under this Division if his or her application for registration and special ballot is received by a returning officer in an electoral district or by the special voting rules administrator after the issue of the writs but before 6:00 p.m. on the 6th day before polling day.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Conditions for voting by special ballot — specified later day
Subsection.Text: If, for the purpose of this subsection, the Chief Electoral Officer specifies on his or her Internet site a day that is after the 6th day before polling day but before polling day, an elector may vote under this Division if his or her application for registration and special ballot is received by the returning officer in the elector’s electoral district or by the special voting rules administrator after the issue of the writs but before 6:00 p.m. on the specified day.

The Chief Electoral Officer may specify a day for the purpose of subsection (2) only if he or she considers that the integrity of the vote will not be affected if applications for registration and special ballots are received after 6:00 p.m. on the 6th day before polling day.
Paragraph 233(1)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the elector’s name and the address of his or her place of ordinary residence;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 233(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An elector who would be under reasonable apprehension of bodily harm if he or she were to indicate the address of his or her place of ordinary residence or his or her mailing address for the purpose of paragraph (1)(a) or (d) may apply to the returning officer or special voting rules administrator to use another address for that purpose. The returning officer or special voting rules administrator, unless he or she considers that it would not be in the public interest to do so, shall grant the application and shall not reveal the addresses in respect of which the application is made except as required to send the special ballot to the elector. For greater certainty, the granting of the application does not change the elector’s place of ordinary residence for the purposes of this Act.
Subsection 233(3) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Section 234 of the Act is repealed.
Sections 236 and 237 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Once an elector’s application for registration and special ballot has been accepted by the returning officer or special voting rules administrator, he or she shall ensure, in accordance with the Chief Election Officer’s instructions,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the elector’s name is entered on the list of electors for the polling division of the elector’s place of ordinary residence, if it is not already included on a list of electors; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that there is an indication on the list of electors that the elector has received a special ballot.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2) and section 237.1, on acceptance of an elector’s application for registration and special ballot, the elector shall be provided with a special ballot.
If section 241 applies, then, on acceptance of an elector’s application for registration and special ballot, the elector shall be given a ballot, an inner envelope and an outer envelope.
Subsections 237.1(3.1) and (3.2) of the Act are repealed.
Paragraph 237.1(4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sections 143 to 144;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 237.1(4) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (d):

Paragraph.Label: (d.1)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 281.6(1) to (4);

Paragraph.Label: (d.2)
Paragraph.Text: section 282.2; and

Section.Text: Sections 238 and 239 of the Act are replaced by the following:
An elector who has been provided with a special ballot may vote only by following the procedure set out in subsections 227(2) and (3).

Section.MarginalNote: Receipt of special ballot — application made outside electoral district

Subsection.Text: In order to have their special ballot counted, an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the special voting rules administrator or by a returning officer outside the elector’s electoral district shall ensure that the special ballot is received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on polling day. The special ballot shall be transmitted by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sending the sealed outer envelope to the special voting rules administrator by mail or any other means; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: delivering it to a Canadian Embassy, High Commission or Consular Office, to a Canadian Forces base outside Canada or to any other place that the Chief Electoral Officer designates.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Receipt of special ballot — application made in electoral district
Subsection.Text: In order to have their special ballot counted, an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the returning officer in the elector’s electoral district shall ensure that the ballot is received at the office of that returning officer before the close of the polling stations on polling day.

Despite subsection (2), a special ballot referred to in that subsection that is received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on polling day shall be counted.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 241:

If, under section 241, an elector is given a ballot that is not a special ballot, no marking shall be placed on the back of the ballot in the space otherwise indicated in Form 3 of Schedule 1 for a polling division number.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 242(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If the ballot or special ballot is incapable of being used, the elector shall return it to the election officer, who shall mark it as a spoiled ballot and give the elector another ballot or special ballot.
Subsection 242(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
An elector shall not be given more than one ballot or special ballot, as the case may be, under subsection (1).
The portion of subsection 243(1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

When an elector personally goes to the office of the returning officer and is unable to read or because of a disability is unable to vote in the manner described in this Division, the designated election officer shall assist the elector by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: completing the declaration referred to in paragraph 227(2)(c) and writing the elector’s name where the elector’s signature is to be written; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 243(1)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: marking the ballot as directed by the elector in the elector’s presence.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 243(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
An election officer who assists an elector under subsection (1) shall indicate, by signing the note on the declaration, that the elector was assisted.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 243:

Section.MarginalNote: Assistance by friend or related person

Subsection.Text: If an elector requires assistance to vote, one of the following persons may accompany the elector into the voting compartment at the office of the returning officer and assist the elector to mark his or her ballot:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a friend of the elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the elector’s spouse or common-law partner; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a relative of the elector or of the elector’s spouse or common-law partner.

Subsection.Text: A person described in subsection (1) who wishes to assist an elector in marking a ballot shall first make a solemn declaration in the prescribed form that he or she

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: will mark the ballot paper in the manner directed by the elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: will not disclose the name of the candidate for whom the elector voted;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: will not try to influence the elector in choosing a candidate; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has not, during the current election, assisted another person, as a friend, to mark a ballot.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 243.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

On application of an elector who is unable to read — or who is unable to vote in the manner described in this Division because of a disability — and who is unable to personally go to the office of the returning officer because of a disability, an election officer shall go to the elector’s dwelling place and, in the presence of a witness who is chosen by the elector, assist the elector by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: completing the declaration referred to in paragraph 227(2)(c) and writing the elector’s name where the elector’s signature is to be written; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 243.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The election officer and the witness who assist an elector under subsection (1) shall indicate, by signing the note on the declaration, that the elector was assisted.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 244:
This Division does not apply to an elector who is incarcerated in a place designated under subsection 205(1) of the National Defence Act.
Subsection 245(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every elector is entitled to vote under this Division on the 12th day before polling day.

Section.Text: Section 246 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The federal and provincial ministers responsible for correctional institutions shall each designate a person as a coordinating officer to work, during and between elections, with the Chief Electoral Officer to carry out the purposes and provisions of this Division.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 247(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Notification of issue of the writs

Subsection.Text: Without delay after the issue of the writs, the Chief Electoral Officer shall inform the federal and provincial ministers responsible for correctional institutions of their issue.
The portion of subsection 247(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
On being informed of the issue of the writs, each minister referred to in subsection (1) shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: inform their respective designated coordinating officer of their issue;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 247(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: inform the Chief Electoral Officer and their respective designated coordinating officer of the name and address of each liaison officer.

Section.Text: Subsection 248(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: During the election period, a liaison officer shall cooperate with the Chief Electoral Officer in the administration of the registration and the taking of the votes of electors, including by informing the Chief Electoral Officer of the identity of the electors who received a special ballot.

Section.Text: Subsection 250(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The polling stations shall be open on the 12th day before polling day from 9:00 a.m. and shall be kept open until every elector who is registered under subsection 251(1) has voted, but in no case shall they be kept open later than 8:00 p.m. on that day.

Section.Text: Subsection 251(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Application for registration and special ballot

Subsection.Text: Before the 12th day before polling day, each liaison officer shall ensure that an application for registration and special ballot in the prescribed form is completed for every eligible elector of the correctional institution who wishes to vote, indicating his or her place of ordinary residence as determined under subsection (2).
Subsection 253(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Before the 18th day before polling day, each returning officer shall, for each correctional institution in his or her electoral district, in consultation with the liaison officer for the institution, establish one or more polling stations and assign at least two election officers to each polling station.
Paragraph 253(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide, as necessary, the materials to the election officers who are assigned under subsection (1) to a polling station for the correctional institution; and

Section.Text: The portion of section 254 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
On the day on which the electors cast their ballots, at each polling station an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall
Section 256 of the Act is replaced by the following:

With the prior authorization of correctional authorities, a Canadian citizen may represent a registered party during the taking of the votes at a correctional institution if he or she provides an election officer who is assigned to a polling station at the correctional institution with an authorization in the prescribed form signed by a candidate for that party, or a copy of one.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 257(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Before delivering a special ballot to an elector, an election officer who is assigned to a polling station at a correctional institution shall require the elector to complete an application for registration and special ballot, if the elector has not already done so, and to sign the declaration on the outer envelope.
The portion of subsection 257(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
After the elector has signed the declaration on the outer envelope, the election officer shall
The portion of subsection 258(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The elector shall vote by writing on the special ballot the name of the candidate of his or her choice, folding it and, in the presence of the election officer,
Subsection 258(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If the special ballot is incapable of being used, the elector shall return it to the election officer, who shall mark it as a spoiled ballot and give the elector another special ballot.
The portion of subsection 259(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If an elector is unable to read or because of a disability is unable to vote in the manner described in this Division, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall assist the elector by
Paragraph 259(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: marking the special ballot as directed by the elector in his or her presence and in the presence of another election officer who is assigned to the polling station.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 259(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The election officers shall sign a note on the outer envelope indicating that the elector was assisted.

Section.Text: The portion of section 260 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Delivery of documents after the vote
Section.Text: Without delay after the votes have been cast at a correctional institution, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall deliver to the liaison officer for the institution
Section 261 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Deadline for return of election material
Section.Text: Every liaison officer shall ensure that the election material referred to in section 260 is received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region no later than 6:00 p.m. on polling day.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 267(1) of the Act before paragraph (c) is replaced by the following:
The special ballot officers shall set aside an inner envelope unopened if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information concerning the elector, as set out in the declaration referred to in paragraph 227(2)(c) or subsection 257(1), does not correspond with the information on the application for registration and special ballot;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a declaration referred to in paragraph (a) or in section 212 — other than one in respect of an elector who has voted with assistance under section 216, 243 or 259 — does not bear the elector’s signature;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 267(1)(c) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the correct electoral district of the elector whose ballot is contained in the inner envelope cannot be ascertained;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 267(1)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the outer envelope has been received by the special voting rules administrator in the National Capital Region after 6:00 p.m. on polling day; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 267(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Procedure when elector votes more than once
Subsection.Text: If, after receiving an elector’s outer envelope but before counting the inner envelopes, the special ballot officers ascertain that the elector has voted more than once, they shall set the elector’s inner envelope aside unopened.
The portion of subsection 267(3) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disposition of inner envelopes that are set aside
Subsection.Text: When an inner envelope is set aside unopened as described in subsection (1) or (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it shall be endorsed by the special voting rules administrator with the reason why it has been set aside;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 267(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an inner envelope set aside under subsection (1), the ballot contained in it is deemed to be a spoiled ballot.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 267(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
When an inner envelope is set aside unopened as described in this section, it shall be kept with the outer envelope and — if it did not appear on the outer envelope — the declaration.
If a dispute arises regarding the setting aside of inner envelopes, it shall be referred to the special voting rules administrator, whose decision is final.
The special voting rules administrator shall prepare a report in respect of the number of inner envelopes that are set aside under this Division.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 272(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all other documents and election materials received from commanding officers and election officers;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the solemn declarations made under subsection 23(1); and

Section.Text: Sections 273 and 274 of the Act are replaced by the following:

The returning officer shall, as soon as possible, notify the candidates of the names of the election officers who he or she assigns to verify the declarations referred to in paragraph 227(2)(c) and to count the special ballots issued to electors in his or her electoral district and received at his or her office.

A candidate or his or her representative may be present for the verification of the declarations referred to in paragraph 227(2)(c) and the counting of ballots received at the returning officer’s office.
Subsection 275(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The returning officer shall ensure that the ballots received at his or her office are kept sealed until they are given to an election officer referred to in section 273.
Subsection 276(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The election officers referred to in section 273 shall verify the declarations made under paragraph 227(2)(c), at the time fixed by the Chief Electoral Officer and in accordance with his or her instructions, by determining from the information in the declaration whether the elector is entitled to vote in the electoral district.
Subsection 276(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Provision of materials to election officer
Subsection.Text: One of the election officers shall be provided with the applications for registration and special ballot received before the deadline, along with any other materials that may be required.
The portion of subsection 277(1) of the Act before paragraph (d) is replaced by the following:
An election officer referred to in section 273 shall set aside an elector’s inner envelope unopened if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information concerning the elector, as set out in the elector’s declaration made under paragraph 227(2)(c), does not correspond with the information on the application for registration and special ballot;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the declaration — other than one in respect of an elector who has voted with assistance under section 243 or 243.1 — does not bear the elector’s signature;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the elector has voted more than once; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 277(1)(d) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: l’enveloppe extérieure est reçue après le délai fixé.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 277(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:
When the declarations are verified, an election officer referred to in section 273 shall register any objection to an elector’s right to vote in the electoral district in the prescribed form.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Noting of reasons for setting aside
Subsection.Text: When an elector’s inner envelope is set aside unopened as described in subsection (1), the election officer who set it aside shall note on it the reasons for the setting aside. The election officer and another election officer referred to in section 273 shall both initial the envelope.
When an elector’s inner envelope is set aside unopened as described in this section, it shall be kept with the outer envelope and — if it did not appear on the outer envelope — the declaration.

Section.Text: Section 278 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The election officers referred to in section 273 shall count all inner envelopes that have not been set aside.
They shall put all the inner envelopes that have not been set aside in a ballot box provided by the returning officer.

After the close of the polling stations, one of the election officers shall open the ballot box and, together with another of the election officers, shall open the inner envelopes and count the votes.
The portion of subsection 279(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An election officer shall, in counting the ballots, reject a ballot if
Subsections 279(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:
An election officer shall not reject a special ballot for the sole reason that the elector has incorrectly written the name of a candidate, if the ballot clearly indicates the elector’s intent.

An election officer shall not reject a special ballot for the sole reason that the elector has written, in addition to the name of a candidate, the candidate’s political affiliation, if the ballot clearly indicates the elector’s intent.

Section.Text: The headings before section 281 and sections 281 and 282 of the Act are replaced by the following:
The provisions of this Part apply inside and outside Canada.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall not vote at an election.

Section.MarginalNote: Inducing Chief Electoral Officer to vote
Section.Text: No person shall induce or attempt to induce the Chief Electoral Officer to vote at an election knowing that it is the Chief Electoral Officer whom he or she is inducing or attempting to induce to vote and that the Chief Electoral Officer is prohibited from voting.
No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: vote or attempt to vote at an election knowing that he or she

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is not a Canadian citizen when he or she votes, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is not 18 years of age or older — or will not be 18 years of age or older — on polling day; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: induce or attempt to induce another person to vote at an election knowing that the other person

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is not a Canadian citizen — or will not be a Canadian citizen — when he or she votes, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is not 18 years of age or older — or will not be 18 years of age or older — on polling day.

Section.MarginalNote: Not ordinarily resident in electoral district
Section.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: vote or attempt to vote at an election in a particular electoral district knowing that his or her place of ordinary residence is not in that electoral district; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: induce or attempt to induce another person to vote at an election in a particular electoral district knowing that the other person’s place of ordinary residence is not in that electoral district.

Section.MarginalNote: Voting more than once — general election

Subsection.Text: No person who has voted in a general election shall vote again, or attempt to vote again, in that general election.
No person who has voted in a by-election shall vote again, or attempt to vote again, at that by-election or at any other by-election that is held on the same day.
Every person present at a polling station or at the counting of the votes shall maintain the secrecy of the vote.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Attempting to obtain information about elector’s vote
Subsection.Text: Except as provided by this Act, no person shall, in a polling station, attempt to obtain any information as to the candidate for whom any elector is about to vote or has voted.
Except as provided by this Act, no person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on entering the polling station and before receiving a ballot or special ballot, openly declare for whom he or she intends to vote;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: while in the polling station, show his or her ballot or special ballot, when marked, so as to allow the name of the candidate for whom he or she has voted to be seen; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: before leaving the polling station, openly declare for whom he or she has voted.

Subsection.Text: No person who has seen a ballot or special ballot that has been marked by an elector shall disclose information as to how it was marked unless he or she is the elector who marked it or he or she has been authorized to make the disclosure by the elector who marked it.

No person shall, at the counting of the votes, attempt to obtain information or communicate information obtained at the counting as to the candidate for whom a vote is given in a particular ballot or special ballot.
No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: request or apply for a ballot or special ballot in a name that is not his or her own;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: vote using a forged ballot or forged special ballot;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: request or apply for a ballot or special ballot to which he or she is not entitled;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide a ballot or special ballot to any person when not authorized under this Act to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: have a ballot or special ballot in his or her possession when not authorized under this Act to do so;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: alter, deface or destroy a ballot, the initials of the election officer that are signed on a ballot or the number of the polling division or advance polling district that is marked on a ballot;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: put or cause to be put into a ballot box a ballot, special ballot or other paper otherwise than as provided by this Act or by instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: take a ballot out of the polling station or the office of the returning officer otherwise than as provided by this Act or by instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer; or

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: destroy, take, open or otherwise interfere with a ballot box or book or packet of ballots or special ballots otherwise than as provided by this Act or by instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subsection.Text: No election officer shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: with the intent of causing the reception of a vote that should not have been cast or the non-reception of a vote that should have been cast, put his or her initials on the back of any paper purporting to be or capable of being used as a ballot at an election; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: place on a ballot or special ballot any writing, number or mark with intent that the elector to whom the ballot or special ballot is to be given, or has been given, can be identified.

Subsection.Text: No unit election officer shall place on a special ballot any writing, number or mark with intent that the elector to whom the special ballot is to be given, or has been given, can be identified.

Section.MarginalNote: Photograph, video or copy of marked ballot

Subsection.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: take a photograph or make a video recording of a ballot or special ballot that has been marked, at an election, by an elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make a copy, in any manner, of any ballot or special ballot that has been marked, at an election, by an elector; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: distribute or show, in any manner, to one or more persons, a photograph, video recording or copy of a ballot or special ballot that has been marked, at an election, by an elector.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply to any person with a visual impairment who takes a photograph or makes a video recording or copy of a ballot or special ballot that he or she has marked for the purpose of verifying the accuracy of their marking.
Subsection (1) does not apply to a person who does anything referred to in that subsection for the purpose of a recount under Part 14 or for the purpose of any other legal proceeding.

Section.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: make a false statement in an application for registration and special ballot; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make a false statement in a declaration signed by him or her before an election officer or unit election officer.

Subsection.Text: At an election, no person shall, as a friend, assist under section 155 or 243.01, more than one elector for the purpose of marking a ballot.
No person who assists an elector under section 155 or 243.01 shall, directly or indirectly, disclose the name of the candidate for whom the elector voted or that candidate’s political affiliation.

Section.MarginalNote: Vouching for more than one person

Subsection.Text: No person shall vouch for more than one person at an election, except in the cases referred to in subsections 143(3.01), 161(2) and 169(2.01).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Voucher not qualified as elector, etc.
Subsection.Text: No person shall vouch for another person if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person who vouches is not qualified as an elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person who vouches does not personally know the other person; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the person who vouches does not reside in a polling division assigned to the same polling station as the polling division in which the other person resides or, in the cases referred to in subsections 143(3.01), 161(2) and 169(2.01), in a polling division in the other person’s electoral district or an adjacent electoral district.

Subsection.Text: No person who has been vouched for at an election shall vouch for another person at that election.

Section.Text: No person shall, in a polling station or in any place where voting at an election is taking place, influence or attempt to influence electors to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the election.

Section.MarginalNote: Influencing electors — election officers and staff of returning officers
Section.Text: Subject to section 141, no election officer, unit election officer or member of the staff of a returning officer shall, while exercising their powers or performing their duties, influence or attempt to influence an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election.

Subsection.Text: No person or entity referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) shall, during an election period, unduly influence an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the election:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who is not a Canadian citizen or a permanent resident as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act and who does not reside in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or entity incorporated, formed or otherwise organized outside Canada that does not carry on business in Canada or whose primary purpose in Canada during an election period is to influence electors during that period to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the election;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trade union that does not hold bargaining rights for employees in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign political party; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign government or an agent or mandatary of a foreign government.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), a person or entity unduly influences an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they knowingly incur any expense to directly promote or oppose a candidate in that election, a registered party that has endorsed a candidate in that election or the leader of such a registered party;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one of the things done by them to influence the elector is an offence under an Act of Parliament or a regulation made under any such Act, or under an Act of the legislature of a province or a regulation made under any such Act.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, subsection (1) does not apply if the only thing done by the person or entity to influence the elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for the particular candidate or registered party, consists of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an expression of their opinion about the outcome or desired outcome of the election;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement by them that encourages the elector to vote or refrain from voting for any candidate or registered party in the election; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission to the public through broadcasting, or through electronic or print media, of an editorial, a debate, a speech, an interview, a column, a letter, a commentary or news, regardless of the expense incurred in doing so, if no contravention of subsection 330(1) or (2) is involved in the transmission.

Subsection.Text: No person or entity shall act in collusion with a person or entity to whom subsection (1) applies for the purpose of contravening that subsection.

No person or entity shall sell any advertising space to a person or entity to whom subsection (1) applies for the purpose of enabling that person or entity to transmit an election advertising message or to cause an election advertising message to be transmitted.

Section.Text: No person shall interfere with, or attempt to interfere with, an elector who is marking a ballot or special ballot.
No person shall prevent or attempt to prevent an elector from voting at an election.

Subsection.Text: No person shall, during an election period, directly or indirectly, offer a bribe to influence an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the election.

No person shall, during an election period, accept or agree to accept a bribe that is offered to them to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the election.

Section.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by intimidation or duress, compel or attempt to compel a person to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: by any pretence or contrivance, including by representing that the ballot or special ballot or the manner of voting at an election is not secret, influence or attempt to influence a person to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 283(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:
Immediately after the close of a polling station, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall count the votes in the presence of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: another election officer who is assigned to the polling station; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any candidates or their representatives who are present or, if no candidates or representatives are present, at least two electors.

Subsection.Text: One of those election officers shall supply all the persons referred to in paragraph (1)(b) who are present and who request one with a tally sheet to keep their own score of the voting.
The portion of subsection 283(3) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
The election officer who counts the votes shall, in the following order,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: count the number of electors who voted, count the number of those to whom a certificate was given under subsection 161(4), make an entry at the end of the list of electors that states “The number of electors who voted at this election is (stating the number). Of these, the number of electors to whom a certificate was given under subsection 161(4) is (stating the number).”, sign the list and place the list in the envelope supplied for that purpose;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 283(3)(d) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: total the number of electors indicated under paragraph (a) who voted and the numbers arrived at in paragraphs (b) and (c) in order to ascertain that all ballots that were provided by the returning officer are accounted for;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 283(3)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: examine each ballot, show the ballot to each person who is present, and ask the election officer referred to in paragraph (1)(a) to make a note on the tally sheet beside the name of the candidate for whom the vote was cast for the purpose of arriving at the total number of votes cast for each candidate.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 284(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
In examining the ballots, the election officer who counts the votes shall reject one
Subsections 284(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

No ballot shall be rejected by reason only that an election officer placed on it any writing, number or mark, or failed to remove the counterfoil or to write the elector’s polling division number on the back of the ballot.

If a ballot is found with the counterfoil attached, the election officer who counts the votes shall, while concealing the number on it from all persons present and without examining it, remove and destroy the counterfoil.

Section.Text: Sections 285 and 286 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Ballots not initialled by election officer
Section.Text: If the election officer who counts the votes determines that a ballot has not been initialed by an election officer, he or she shall, in the presence of the election officer referred to in paragraph 283(1)(a) and witnesses, initial and count the ballot if satisfied that all ballots that were provided by the returning officer have been accounted for, as described in paragraph 283(3)(d).

Subsection.Text: One of the election officers referred to in subsection 283(1) shall make a record, in the prescribed form, of every objection to a ballot made by a candidate or candidate’s representative, give a number to the objection, write that number on the ballot and initial it.

Every question that is raised by the objection shall be decided by the election officer who counts the votes, and the decision is subject to reversal only on a recount or on application under subsection 524(1).
Subsection 287(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The election officer who counts the votes shall prepare a statement of the vote, in the prescribed form, that sets out the number of votes in favour of each candidate and the number of rejected ballots and shall place the original statement and a copy of it in the separate envelopes supplied for the purpose.
Subsection 287(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
The election officer shall give a copy of the statement of the vote to each of the candidates’ representatives present at the count.
Subsections 288(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

One of the election officers referred to in subsection 283(1) shall place the ballots for each candidate into separate envelopes, write on each envelope the name of the candidate and the number of votes he or she received, and seal it. The election officers shall sign the seal on each envelope, and the witnesses may also sign them.
One of the election officers shall place into separate envelopes the rejected ballots, the registration certificates and the list of electors, and shall seal the envelopes.
The portion of subsection 288(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
One of the election officers shall seal in a large envelope supplied for the purpose
Subsection 288(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
The ballot box shall be sealed with the seals provided by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Section.Text: Sections 288.01 and 288.1 of the Act are replaced by the following:

One of the election officers referred to in subsection 283(1) shall place the form for each solemn declaration made under subsection 143(3) or paragraph 161(1)(b) or 169(2)(b) in an envelope supplied for the purpose.

Section.MarginalNote: Periodic statements of electors who voted
Section.Text: One of the election officers referred to in subsection 283(1) shall place a copy of each document prepared for the purpose of paragraph 162(i.1) in an envelope supplied for the purpose.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 289(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Counting of votes on polling day

Subsection.Text: At least two election officers who are assigned to an advance polling station and who are specified in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions shall, at the close of the polling stations on polling day, attend at the place mentioned in the notice of advance poll in subparagraph 172(a)(iii) to count the votes.
Paragraph 289(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the application of paragraph 283(3)(e), the two or more election officers who are specified in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions shall open the ballot boxes and empty their contents onto a table; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 289(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (4), no person shall make a count of the votes cast at an advance poll before the close of voting hours on polling day.

The two or more election officers who are specified in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions may begin counting the votes cast at an advance poll one hour before the close of voting hours on polling day if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the returning officer who is responsible for the advance polling station has obtained the Chief Electoral Officer’s prior approval for the counting to begin;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the counting is done in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the counting is done in a manner that ensures the integrity of the vote; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the counting is done in the presence of any candidates or their representatives who are present or, if no candidates or representatives are present, at least two electors.

Section.Text: Section 290 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Sending ballot boxes and envelopes to returning officer
Section.Text: An election officer who is assigned to a polling station or an advance polling station shall, without delay after sealing the ballot box, send to the returning officer the box, with the envelope that contains the original statement of the vote, the envelope that contains the registration certificates, the envelope referred to in section 288.01 and, in the case of an election officer who is assigned to a polling station, the envelope referred to in section 288.1.
Section 292.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: List of persons who made solemn declaration
Section.Text: A returning officer, on the receipt of each envelope referred to in section 288.01, shall create a list of the names of all persons who made a solemn declaration under subsection 143(3) or paragraph 161(1)(b) or 169(2)(b) and shall include in the list the address of each of those persons.
Paragraphs 296(2)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for ascertaining the total number of votes under paragraph (a), may summon any election officer or other person to appear before him or her at a fixed date and time and to bring with them all necessary documents; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may question the election officer or other person respecting the matter in question and, if necessary, ask them to make a solemn declaration in respect of the matter.

Subsection.Text: Section 301 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
The returning officer shall notify each candidate or his or her official agent in writing of the application for a recount.
The portion of subsection 301(2) of the French version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Le juge fixe la date du dépouillement s’il appert, d’après l’affidavit d’un témoin digne de foi, que l’une ou l’autre des situations suivantes existe :
Paragraph 301(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an election officer has incorrectly counted or rejected any ballots, or has written an incorrect number on the statement of the vote for the votes cast for a candidate; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 304(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The judge shall conduct the recount by adding the number of votes reported in the statements of the vote or by counting the valid ballots or all of the ballots returned by election officers or the Chief Electoral Officer.
Subsection 304(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:
In the case of a recount conducted by counting the valid ballots or all of the ballots returned by election officers or the Chief Electoral Officer, the procedure set out in Schedule 4 applies.
Subsection 304(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of conducting a recount, a judge has the power to summon any election officer as a witness and to require him or her to give evidence on oath and, for that purpose, has the same power that is vested in any court of record.

Section.Text: Section 308 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by replacing paragraph (c) with the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: return to the returning officer the election documents or election materials brought under subsection 300(4) or 301(4) for the purpose of the recount; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: deliver to the returning officer the reports created in the course of the recount.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 311(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
La requête peut être appuyée par un affidavit, qu’il n’est pas nécessaire d’intituler d’aucune manière, exposant les faits qui se rattachent au défaut de conformité.
Subsection 311(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Le juge visé ou toute partie intéressée peuvent déposer au bureau du greffier, du registraire ou du protonotaire du tribunal du juge auquel la requête a été présentée des affidavits en réponse à ceux que le requérant a produits; sur demande, ils en fournissent des copies au requérant.
Paragraph 314(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a report of the returning officer’s proceedings in the prescribed form including his or her comments with respect to the state of the election documents received from election officers;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 314(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: all other documents that were used at the election, including documents prepared under paragraph 162(i.1).

Subsection.Text: The definitions election advertising and election survey in section 319 of the Act are repealed.
Section 319 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: online platform includes an Internet site or Internet application whose owner or operator, in the course of their commercial activities, sells, directly or indirectly, advertising space on the site or application to persons or groups. (plateforme en ligne)

Section.Text: Section 321 of the Act is repealed.
Subsection 323(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No person shall transmit election advertising to the public in an electoral district on polling day before the close of all of the polling stations in the electoral district.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 325:

Section.MarginalNote: Online platforms that are subject to requirements

Subsection.Text: This section and section 325.2 apply to any online platform that, in the 12 months before the first day of the pre-election period, in the case of the publication on the platform of a partisan advertising message, or the 12 months before the first day of the election period, in the case of the publication on the platform of an election advertising message, was visited or used by Internet users in Canada an average of at least the following numbers of times per month:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 3,000,000 times, if the content of the online platform is available mainly in English;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 1,000,000 times, if the content of the online platform is available mainly in French; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 100,000 times, if the content of the online platform is available mainly in a language other than English or French.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Registry of partisan advertising messages and election advertising messages
Subsection.Text: The owner or operator of an online platform that sells, directly or indirectly, advertising space to the following persons and groups shall publish on the platform a registry of the persons’ and groups’ partisan advertising messages and election advertising messages published on the platform during that period:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a registered party or eligible party;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a registered association;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a nomination contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a potential candidate or a candidate; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a third party that is required to register under subsection 349.6(1) or 353(1).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Information to be included in registry
Subsection.Text: The registry referred to in subsection (2) shall include the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an electronic copy of each partisan advertising message and each election advertising message published on the platform; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for each advertising message referred to in paragraph (a), the name of the person who authorized the advertising message’s publication on the platform, namely

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered agent of the registered party or eligible party, in the case of an advertising message whose publication was requested by a registered party or eligible party,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the financial agent of the registered association, in the case of an advertising message whose publication was requested by a registered association,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the financial agent of the nomination contestant, in the case of an advertising message whose publication was requested by a nomination contestant,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the official agent of the potential candidate or candidate, in the case of an advertising message whose publication was requested by a potential candidate or a candidate, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the financial agent of the registered third party, in the case of an advertising message whose publication was requested by a registered third party.

Subsection.Text: The owner or operator of the online platform shall publish in the registry referred to in subsection (2) the information referred to in subsection (3) for each partisan advertising message and each election advertising message during the following periods:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a partisan advertising message, during the period that begins on the day on which the online platform first publishes the advertising message and ends two years after

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the end of the election period of the general election immediately following the pre-election period, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the day referred to in paragraph (b) of the definition pre-election period if there is no general election immediately following the pre-election period; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an election advertising message, during the period that begins on the first day on which the online platform publishes such an advertising message and ends two years after the end of the election period.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Information to be kept after publication period
Subsection.Text: The owner or operator of the online platform shall keep the information that was included in the registry referred to in subsection (2) in respect of each partisan advertising message and each election advertising message for five years after the end of the applicable publication period referred to in subsection (4).

Section.MarginalNote: Information to be provided by advertiser
Section.Text: A person or group referred to in any of paragraphs 325.1(2)(a) to (e) that requests the publication of a partisan advertising message or election advertising message on an online platform shall provide the owner or operator of the platform with all the information in the person’s or group’s control that the owner or operator needs in order to comply with subsection 325.1(2).
The heading before section 326 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection 326(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (e), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (f) and by adding the following after paragraph (f):

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the Internet site on which a report referred to in subsection (3) is published.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 326(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

In addition to the information referred to in subsection (1), in the case of a transmission to the public by means other than broadcasting, the wording of the survey questions in respect of which data is obtained must be provided.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of transmission of survey results
Subsection.Text: The first person who transmits to the public the results of an election survey — other than a survey that is described in section 327 — shall, if that person is not the sponsor of the survey, ensure that the sponsor of the survey has been notified, before the transmission, of the date on which the results are to be transmitted.
The portion of subsection 326(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A sponsor of an election survey — other than a survey that is described in section 327 — shall, during an election period, ensure that a report on the results of the survey is published and remains for the remainder of that period on an Internet site that is available to the public. The sponsor shall do so before transmitting the results of the survey, if they are the first person to transmit them or, if they are not, as soon as feasible after being notified of the date of transmission under subsection (2.1), and the report shall include the following, as applicable:
Subsection 326(4) of the Act is repealed.

Section.Text: Subsection 328(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — causing transmission of election survey results during blackout period

Subsection.Text: No person shall cause to be transmitted to the public, in an electoral district on polling day before the close of all of the polling stations in that electoral district, the results of an election survey that have not previously been transmitted to the public.

Section.Text: Subsection 330(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — use of broadcasting station outside Canada

Subsection.Text: No person shall, with intent to influence persons to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election, use a broadcasting station outside Canada, or aid, abet, counsel or procure the use of a broadcasting station outside Canada, during an election period, for the broadcasting of any matter having reference to an election.
Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of any matter that is broadcast if the broadcasting signals originated in Canada.

Section.Text: Section 331 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 348.06(2)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the person or group that is a party to the agreement;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 348.06(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the person or group on whose behalf calls will be made under the agreement; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 348.07(2)(b) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the person or group that is a party to the agreement;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 348.07(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the name of the person or group on whose behalf calls will be made under the agreement; and

Section.Text: Section 348.12 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: As soon as feasible but no later than 30 days after polling day, the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission shall, in the manner that it considers appropriate, publish the registration notices relating to the election that have been filed with it.

Nothing in subsection (1) precludes the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission from publishing, before polling day, any registration notice filed with it or any information related to any incomplete registration notice that is filed with it.

Section.Text: The heading of Division 2 of Part 16.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Scripts, Recordings and Lists of Telephone Numbers

Section.Text: Section 348.16 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a list of every telephone number called under the agreement during the election period.

Section.Text: Section 348.17 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a list of every telephone number called under the agreement during the election period.

Section.Text: Sections 348.18 and 348.19 of the Act are replaced by the following:

If, during an election period, a person or group uses their internal services to make calls by means of an automatic dialing-announcing device for any purpose relating to the election, including a purpose referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) of the definition voter contact calling services in section 348.01, the person or group shall keep, for one year after the end of the election period,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a recording of each unique message conveyed by the device and a record of every date on which it was so conveyed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a list of every telephone number called for that purpose during the election period.

Section.MarginalNote: Third party that is corporation or group — internal services
Section.Text: If, during an election period, a third party that is a corporation or group uses its internal services to make live voice calls for any purpose relating to the election, including a purpose referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) of the definition voter contact calling services in section 348.01, the third party shall, if a script is used, keep, for one year after the end of the election period,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a copy of each unique script used and a record of every date on which the script was used; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a list of every telephone number called for that purpose during the election period.

Section.Text: The heading of Part 17 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Third Party Advertising, Partisan Activities and Election Surveys

Subsection.Text: The definitions election advertising and election advertising expense in section 349 of the Act are repealed.
The definition third party in section 349 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: third party means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in Division 0.1, a person or a group other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: during an election period, a candidate, a registered party or an electoral district association of a registered party, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: outside of an election period,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a registered party or eligible party or a registered association,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a potential candidate within the meaning of paragraph (a), (b) or (d) of the definition potential candidate in subsection 2(1), or

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: in Division 1, a person or a group other than

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered party or eligible party or a registered association,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a potential candidate within the meaning of paragraph (a), (b) or (d) of the definition potential candidate in subsection 2(1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a nomination contestant; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in Division 2, a person or a group other than a candidate, a registered party or an electoral district association of a registered party. (tiers)

Subsection.Text: Section 349 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: election survey means an election survey that is conducted by, or caused to be conducted by, a third party — a person or group other than a political party that is registered under an Act of a province — during a pre-election period or an election period and whose results the person or group takes into account

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in deciding whether or not to organize and carry out partisan activities or to transmit partisan advertising messages or election advertising messages; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in their organization and carrying out of those activities or their transmission of those messages. (sondage électoral)

Definition.Text: election survey expense means an expense incurred in respect of the conducting of an election survey

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in Division 1, during a pre-election period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in Division 2, during an election period; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in Division 3, during either a pre-election period or an election period. (dépenses de sondage électoral)

Definition.Text: partisan activity means an activity, including canvassing door-to-door, making telephone calls to electors and organizing rallies, that is carried out by a third party — a person or group other than a political party that is registered under an Act of a province — and that promotes or opposes a registered party or eligible party or the election of a potential candidate, nomination contestant, candidate or leader of a registered party or eligible party, otherwise than by taking a position on an issue with which any such party or person is associated. It does not include election advertising, partisan advertising or a fundraising activity. (activité partisane)
partisan activity expense means an expense incurred in respect of the organization and carrying out of a partisan activity. (dépenses d’activité partisane)
registered third party means a third party that is registered under section 349.6 or 353. (tiers enregistré)

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 349:

Heading.Label: DIVISION 0.1
Heading.TitleText: Prohibition on Use of Foreign Funds by Third Parties

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Division.

Definition.Text: advertising means the transmission to the public by any means of an advertising message that promotes or opposes a registered party or eligible party or the election of a potential candidate, nomination contestant, candidate or leader of a registered party or eligible party, otherwise than by taking a position on an issue with which any such party or person is associated. For greater certainty, it does not include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission to the public of an editorial, a debate, a speech, an interview, a column, a letter, a commentary or news;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the distribution of a book, or the promotion of the sale of a book, for no less than its commercial value, if the book was planned to be made available to the public regardless of whether there was to be an election;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of a document by a Senator or a member the expense of which is paid by the Senate or House of Commons;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission of a document directly by a person or a group to their members, employees or shareholders, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the transmission by an individual, on a non-commercial basis on the Internet, of his or her personal political views; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the making of telephone calls to electors only to encourage them to vote. (publicité)

Definition.Text: foreign entity includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an individual who is not a Canadian citizen or a permanent resident as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or entity incorporated, formed or otherwise organized outside Canada that does not carry on business in Canada or whose only activity carried on in Canada consists of doing anything to influence electors to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trade union that does not hold bargaining rights for employees in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign political party; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign government or an agent or mandatary of one. (entité étrangère)

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of the definition advertising, promoting or opposing includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to a registered party or eligible party,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: naming it,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: identifying it, including by its logo, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: providing a link to an Internet page that does anything referred to in subparagraphs (i) and (ii); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to the election of a potential candidate, nomination contestant, candidate or leader of a registered party or eligible party,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: naming him or her,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: showing a photograph, cartoon or drawing of him or her,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: identifying him or her, including by political affiliation or by any logo that he or she has, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: providing a link to an Internet page that does anything referred to in subparagraphs (i) to (iii).

Section.Text: No third party shall use funds for a partisan activity, for advertising, for election advertising or for an election survey if the source of the funds is a foreign entity.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — circumventing prohibition on use of foreign funds
Section.Text: No third party shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: circumvent, or attempt to circumvent, the prohibition under section 349.02; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: act in collusion with another person or entity for that purpose.

Heading.Label: DIVISION 1
Heading.TitleText: Partisan Activities, Partisan Advertising and Election Surveys During Pre-election Period

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 349.4, a third party shall not incur the following expenses in an aggregate amount of more than $700,000:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: partisan activity expenses in relation to partisan activities that are carried out during a pre-election period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: partisan advertising expenses in relation to partisan advertising messages that are transmitted during that period; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: election survey expenses in relation to election surveys that are conducted during that period.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum pre-election period expenses — electoral district
Subsection.Text: Not more than $7,000 of the maximum amount referred to in subsection (1) shall be incurred to promote or oppose the election of one or more potential candidates or nomination contestants in a given electoral district.

The maximum amount set out in subsection (2) only applies to an amount incurred with respect to a leader of a registered party or eligible party to the extent that it is incurred to promote or oppose his or her election in an electoral district.
The amounts referred to in subsections (1) and (2) shall be multiplied by the inflation adjustment factor referred to in section 384 that is in effect on the first day of the pre-election period.

Section.Text: A third party shall not circumvent, or attempt to circumvent, a maximum amount set out in section 349.1 in any manner, including by splitting itself into two or more third parties for the purpose of circumventing the maximum amount or acting in collusion with another third party so that their combined partisan activity expenses, partisan advertising expenses and election survey expenses exceed the maximum amount.

Subsection.Text: No third party and no registered party shall act in collusion with each other — including by sharing information — in order to influence the third party in its partisan activities that it carries out during a pre-election period, its partisan advertising or its election surveys that it conducts or causes to be conducted during a pre-election period.

No third party and no potential candidate shall act in collusion with each other — including by sharing information — in order to influence the third party in its partisan activities that it carries out during a pre-election period, its partisan advertising or its election surveys that it conducts or causes to be conducted during a pre-election period.

No third party and no person associated with a potential candidate’s activities undertaken with a view to the potential candidate’s eventual election — including an official agent of a potential candidate who is deemed to be a candidate under section 477 — shall act in collusion with each other — including by sharing information — in order to influence the third party in its partisan activities that it carries out during a pre-election period, its partisan advertising or its election surveys that it conducts or causes to be conducted during a pre-election period.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — spending by foreign third parties

Subsection.Text: A foreign third party shall not incur the following expenses:
