Subsection.Text: A political party shall publish on its Internet site its policy for the protection of personal information referred to in paragraph (2)(k) before its leader applies under this section for the party to become a registered party.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 385:

Section.MarginalNote: Policy for the protection of personal information — parties already registered, etc.

Subsection.Text: Within three months after the day on which this section comes into force, the leader of a political party shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with the party’s policy for the protection of personal information referred to in paragraph 385(2)(k) and the Internet address referred to in paragraph 385(2)(l), if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: before the day on which this section comes into force, the leader of the party has applied under section 385 for the party to become a registered party but, as of that day, the Chief Electoral Officer has not yet informed the leader under subsection 389(1) whether or not the party is eligible for registration under section 387; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the day on which this section comes into force, the party is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an eligible party, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered party.

Subsection.Text: If the leader of the political party does not comply with subsection (1), then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a party referred to in paragraph (1)(a), the party is not eligible for registration under section 387;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a party referred to in subparagraph (1)(b)(i), the party may not become a registered party under section 390; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a party referred to in subparagraph (1)(b)(ii), the Chief Electoral Officer shall implement the procedure for non-voluntary deregistration set out in sections 415, 416 and 418.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deemed inclusion in application for registration
Subsection.Text: If the leader of a political party provides the Chief Electoral Officer with the policy and the address referred to in subsection (1) in compliance with that subsection, or in compliance with section 415, then the policy and the address are deemed, as of the day on which they are provided, to be included in the application for registration referred to in subsection 385(2) in respect of the party.

Section.Text: Section 390 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of section 429.1 and — despite subsection (5) — sections 363 and 367, an eligible party that becomes a registered party under subsection (1) is deemed to have been registered from the first day of the pre-election period — if any — before the election period of the election referred to in that subsection.

Section.Text: Section 394 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Chief Electoral Officer shall maintain a registry of political parties that contains the information referred to in paragraphs 385(2)(a) to (h), (k) and (l) and subsections 396(2) and 418(2).
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 405:

Section.MarginalNote: Publication of changes to policy for the protection of personal information
Section.Text: As soon as feasible after reporting a change in its policy for the protection of personal information to the Chief Electoral Officer in writing under subsection 405(1), a registered party or an eligible party shall publish on its Internet site the updated version of the policy, incorporating the change set out in the report.
Subsection 408(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No person shall make a declaration referred to in paragraph 385(2)(i) or subsection 407(2) that they know is false or misleading.

Section.Text: Section 412 of the Act is renumbered as subsection (1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deregistration — failure to publish updated policy for the protection of personal information
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may deregister a registered party if the party fails to publish an updated version of its policy for the protection of personal information on its Internet site in accordance with section 405.1.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deregistration — failure to continue to have policy for the protection of personal information
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may deregister a registered party if the party fails to continue to have a policy for the protection of personal information referred to in paragraph 385(2)(k).

Section.Text: Section 426 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Expenses incurred by registered agent other than chief agent
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (2), a registered agent — other than the chief agent — of a registered party shall, before incurring the party’s expenses, obtain the written authorization of the chief agent to incur those expenses, and shall incur them only in accordance with that authorization.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 429:

Subsection.Text: The maximum amount that is allowed for partisan advertising expenses of a registered party for a pre-election period is $1,400,000.
The amount referred to in subsection (1) shall be multiplied by the inflation adjustment factor referred to in section 384 that is in effect on the first day of the pre-election period.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — partisan advertising expenses more than maximum amount

Subsection.Text: No chief agent of a registered party shall incur partisan advertising expenses on the party’s behalf of a total amount of more than the maximum amount calculated under section 429.1.

No registered party shall circumvent, or attempt to circumvent, that maximum amount in any manner, including by acting in collusion with a potential candidate for the purpose of his or her engaging in partisan advertising so that the combined total of the following exceeds the maximum amount:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the potential candidate’s partisan advertising expenses that relate to the partisan advertising engaged in in collusion with the party; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the party’s partisan advertising expenses.

Subsection.Text: No third party, within the meaning of paragraph (a.1) of the definition third party in section 349, shall act in collusion with a registered party for the purpose of the registered party’s circumventing that maximum amount.

Section.Text: A registered party, or a person acting on its behalf, that causes partisan advertising to be conducted shall mention in or on the partisan advertising message that its transmission was authorized by one of the party’s registered agents.
Subsection 430(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum expenses: postponement of polling day
Subsection.Text: If the Governor in Council orders the postponement of an election under subsection 59(4) for one or more electoral districts, along with the corresponding extension of the election period, then the maximum amount calculated under subsection (1) for a registered party that has endorsed a candidate in that or any of those electoral districts is increased by adding to it the product of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $0.735 multiplied by the number of names on the preliminary list of electors in the electoral districts governed by the order in which the party has endorsed a candidate, or the number of names on the revised list of electors in those electoral districts, whichever is greater, and divided by the number of days in the election period before it was extended;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the inflation adjustment factor published by the Chief Electoral Officer under section 384 that is in effect on the date of the issue of the writ or writs for the election; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the number of days by which the election period is extended.

Section.Text: Subsection 431(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No registered party and no third party, within the meaning of paragraph (b) of the definition third party in section 349, shall act in collusion with each other for the purpose of the registered party’s circumventing the maximum amount referred to in subsection (1).

Section.Text: Subparagraphs 432(2)(j)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as an election expense, each of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the expenses incurred by the registered party — other than accessibility expenses — whether paid or unpaid, including a statement of expenses incurred for voter contact calling services as defined in section 348.01, provided by a calling service provider as defined in that section, that indicates the name of that provider and the amount of those expenses, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the non-monetary contributions used by the registered party as an election expense, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the accessibility expenses incurred by the registered party;

Section.Text: Subsection 433(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a registered party’s candidates for the most recent general election received at that election at least 2% of the number of valid votes cast, or at least 5% of the number of valid votes cast in the electoral districts in which the registered party endorsed a candidate, the registered party’s chief agent shall, for each quarter — in respect of a fiscal period of the registered party — that follows that general election, beginning with the quarter that immediately follows that general election and ending with the quarter in which polling day at the next general election is held, provide the Chief Electoral Officer with a return that includes the information required under paragraphs 432(2)(a) to (d), (i) and (l).

Section.Text: The heading before section 437 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 437(2) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An election expenses return shall set out
Paragraphs 437(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as an election expense, each of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the expenses incurred by the registered party — other than accessibility expenses — whether paid or unpaid, including a statement of expenses incurred for voter contact calling services as defined in section 348.01, provided by a calling service provider as defined in that section, that indicates the name of that provider and the amount of those expenses, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the non-monetary contributions used by the registered party as an election expense;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the accessibility expenses incurred by the registered party; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a general election held on a day set in accordance with subsection 56.1(2) or section 56.2, the partisan advertising expenses incurred by the registered party in relation to partisan advertising messages transmitted during the pre-election period.

Section.Text: The heading before section 444 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Reimbursement of Election Expenses and Accessibility Expenses

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 444(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On receipt from a registered party of the documents referred to in subsection 437(1), the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide the Receiver General with a certificate that sets out the amount that is the sum of 50% of the registered party’s election expenses, as set out in the return for its general election expenses, that were paid by its registered agents and 90% — to a maximum of $250,000 — of the registered party’s accessibility expenses, as set out in that return, that were paid by its registered agents, if

Section.Text: Section 450 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — incurring partisan advertising expenses, etc.

Subsection.Text: No electoral district association of a registered party shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: incur partisan advertising expenses in relation to partisan advertising messages that promote or oppose a registered party or an eligible party and that are transmitted during a pre-election period; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: transmit or cause to be transmitted, during a pre-election period, partisan advertising messages that promote or oppose a registered party or an eligible party.

Subsection.Text: Despite paragraph (1)(a), an electoral district association of a registered party may incur partisan advertising expenses to the extent that the goods or services that the partisan advertising expense is incurred for are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provided to that party, if permitted under paragraph 364(2)(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sold to that party.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), an electoral district association of a registered party may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: incur partisan advertising expenses for the transmission of partisan advertising messages referred to in paragraph (1)(a) to the extent that those messages are intended to be transmitted solely, or substantially solely, within the association’s electoral district; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: transmit or cause to be transmitted partisan advertising messages referred to in paragraph (1)(b) to the extent that those messages are transmitted solely, or substantially solely, within the association’s electoral district.

Section.Text: A registered association that causes partisan advertising to be conducted shall mention in or on the partisan advertising message that its transmission was authorized by one of the association’s electoral district agents.

Subsection.Text: No electoral district association of a registered party shall incur election expenses.
For the purposes of subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: election expense has the meaning given to that expression by subsection 376(1), except that the reference to “a registered party or a candidate” is to be read as a reference to “an electoral district association”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 376(2) to (4) apply, other than paragraph 376(3)(c), except that the reference to “a registered party or a candidate” in subsection 376(4) is to be read as a reference to “an electoral district association”.

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), an electoral district association of a registered party may incur an election expense to the extent that the property or service that the cost was incurred for or the non-monetary contribution was received for — or the goods or services that were accepted — are

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provided to that party, a registered association of that party or a candidate endorsed by that party, if permitted under paragraph 364(2)(b); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: sold to that party or a candidate endorsed by that party.

Subsection.Text: In the case of a general election that is not held on a day set in accordance with subsection 56.1(2) or section 56.2, or of a by-election, an electoral district association is deemed not to have incurred an election expense for election advertising if, on the issue of the writ or writs, it is not able to cancel the transmission of the election advertising message that the expense is in relation to.

Section.Text: The portion of section 464 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
On or before May 31 of every year a registered association shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with
Subsection 469(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: As soon as a proclamation is issued under section 25 of the Electoral Boundaries Readjustment Act relating to a representation order, an application may be made under section 448 for the registration of an electoral district association for an electoral district that is created by — or whose boundaries are revised as a result of — the order. The electoral district association may be registered at any time on or after the day on which the application is made.

Section.Text: Subsection 475.6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The auditor of a registered association that has, in a fiscal period, accepted contributions of $10,000 or more in total or incurred expenses of $10,000 or more in total shall report to the association’s financial agent on the association’s financial transactions return and shall, in accordance with generally accepted auditing standards, make any examination that will enable the auditor to give an opinion in the report as to whether the return presents fairly the information contained in the financial records on which it is based.

Section.Text: Section 475.8 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The $1,500 amount set out in subsection (1) shall be multiplied by the inflation adjustment factor referred to in section 384 that is in effect on the last day of the registered association’s fiscal period to which the auditor’s report relates.

Section.Text: The heading before section 476 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Section 476 of the Act is replaced by the following:
In this Division, selection date means the date on which a nomination contest is decided. (date de désignation)
Subsection 476.65(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: All of a nomination contestant’s financial transactions in relation to the contestant’s nomination campaign that involve the receipt of money are to be deposited to the account unless the transaction involves the receipt of the contestant’s own funds and those funds are used to pay a litigation expense or a personal expense.

All of a nomination contestant’s financial transactions in relation to the contestant’s nomination campaign that involve the payment of money are to be paid from the account unless the transaction involves the payment out of the contestant’s own funds of a litigation expense or a personal expense.
Subsection 476.66(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
No person or entity, other than a nomination contestant’s financial agent, shall pay the contestant’s nomination campaign expenses except for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a litigation expense;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: travel and living expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a personal expense; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a petty expense referred to in section 381.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 476.66(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibition — paying contestant’s personal expenses, etc.
Subsection.Text: No person or entity, other than the nomination contestant or their financial agent, shall pay the contestant’s expenses referred to in any of paragraphs 4(a) to (c).

Section.Text: The portion of section 476.67 of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
The limit for nomination contest expenses that is allowed for a nomination contestant in an electoral district is the amount

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is 20% of the election expenses limit that was calculated under subsection 477.49(1) for that electoral district during the immediately preceding general election, if the boundaries for the electoral district have not changed since then; or

Section.Text: Subsection 476.68(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: No nomination contestant and no financial agent of a nomination contestant shall incur total nomination contest expenses in an amount that is more than the limit allowed for that electoral district under section 476.67.
Paragraph 476.75(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of nomination contest expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of litigation expenses that includes an indication of which of those expenses were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 476.65(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of travel and living expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of personal expenses that includes an indication of which of those expenses were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 476.65(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them;

Paragraph.Label: (a.4)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of nomination campaign expenses, other than the expenses referred to in paragraphs (a) to (a.3);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 476.75(2)(h) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the funds transferred by the nomination contestant to a registered party, a registered association or a candidate;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 476.75(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Together with the nomination campaign return, the nomination contestant’s financial agent shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with documents evidencing expenses set out in the return, including bank statements, deposit slips, cancelled cheques and the contestant’s written statement referred to in subsection 476.82(1).
Subsection 476.75(9) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Electoral Officer is deemed to have received the declaration for the purpose of section 476.901.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 476.82(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any travel and living expenses paid by the contestant and details of those expenses, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any litigation expenses and personal expenses paid by the contestant, details of those expenses and an indication of which of them were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 476.65(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: declares that the contestant did not pay for any travel and living expenses, litigation expenses or personal expenses.

Subsection.Text: Section 476.82 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
Together with the statement referred to in paragraph (1)(a), the nomination contestant shall send their financial agent documents evidencing the payment of the expenses referred to in the statement.
Section 476.82 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If, after having sent their financial agent the statement referred to in subsection (1), a nomination contestant pays a litigation expense other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 476.65(1), the contestant shall, as soon as feasible after paying it,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notify their financial agent of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: inform their financial agent of the amount of the expense, the details of the expense and the source of the funds used to pay it; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: send their financial agent documents evidencing payment of the expense.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 476.89:
No nomination contestant shall send to their financial agent a declaration referred to in paragraph 476.75(1)(d) that they know or reasonably ought to know is false or misleading.
Section 476.9 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — false, misleading or incomplete document
Section.Text: No financial agent of a nomination contestant shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with a document referred to in subsection 476.75(1), (10), (11), (12) or (15) that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the financial agent knows or ought reasonably to know contains a material statement that is false or misleading; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a document referred to in paragraph 476.75(1)(a), does not substantially set out the information required under subsection 476.75(2) and, in the case of a document referred to in subsection 476.75(10), (11), (12) or (15), does not substantially set out the information required under that subsection.

Subsection.Text: On receipt of the documents referred to in subsection 476.75(1), including the auditor’s report, and a copy of the auditor’s invoice for the report, the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide the Receiver General with a certificate that sets out the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the expenses incurred for the audit, up to a maximum of the lesser of 3% of the nomination contestant’s nomination contest expenses and $1,500, and

Subsection.Text: On receipt of the certificate, the Receiver General shall pay the amount set out in it to the auditor out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

The $1,500 amount set out in paragraph (1)(a) and the $250 amount set out in paragraph (1)(b) shall be multiplied by the inflation adjustment factor referred to in section 384 that is in effect on the selection date.

Section.Text: Section 476.91 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The surplus amount of nomination campaign funds that a nomination contestant receives for a nomination contest is the amount by which the sum of the following is more than the sum of the contestant’s nomination campaign expenses paid from the bank account referred to in subsection 476.65(1) and any transfers referred to in paragraph 364(5)(a):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contributions accepted by the financial agent on behalf of the contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: money received from the sale referred to in subsection (2); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other amounts received by the contestant for their nomination campaign that are required to be deposited into the bank account referred to in subsection 476.65(1) and are not repayable.

Subsection.Text: Before the surplus amount of nomination campaign funds is disposed of in accordance with sections 476.92 and 476.93, a nomination contestant’s financial agent shall sell, at their fair market value, any capital assets whose acquisition constitutes a nomination campaign expense.

Section.Text: Section 477 of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of Division 1 of this Part and this Division, except sections 477.89 to 477.95, a candidate is deemed to have been a candidate from the time they accept a provision of goods or services under section 364, accept a transfer of funds under that section, accept a contribution, borrow money under section 373 or incur an electoral campaign expense within the meaning of subsection 375(1).
Section 477.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A candidate shall appoint an official agent before accepting a provision of goods or services under section 364, accepting a transfer of funds under that section, accepting a contribution, borrowing money under section 373 or incurring an electoral campaign expense within the meaning of subsection 375(1).
A candidate shall appoint an auditor without delay if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they accept contributions of $10,000 or more in total;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: they incur electoral campaign expenses of $10,000 or more in total; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: they receive 10% or more of the valid votes cast in the electoral district in which they are a candidate.

Section.Text: Subsection 477.46(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: All of a candidate’s financial transactions in relation to the candidate’s electoral campaign that involve the receipt of money are to be deposited to the account unless the transaction involves the receipt of the candidate’s own funds and those funds are used to pay a litigation expense or a personal expense.

All of a candidate’s financial transactions in relation to the candidate’s electoral campaign that involve the payment of money are to be paid from the account unless the transaction involves the payment out of the candidate’s own funds of a litigation expense or a personal expense.
Subsection 477.47(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
No person or entity, other than a candidate’s official agent, shall pay the candidate’s electoral campaign expenses except for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a litigation expense;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: travel and living expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a personal expense; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a petty expense referred to in section 381.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 477.47(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (5), a candidate shall, before incurring election expenses, obtain the written authorization of their official agent to incur those expenses, and shall incur them only in accordance with that authorization.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibition — paying candidate’s personal expenses, etc.
Subsection.Text: No person or entity, other than a candidate or their official agent, shall pay the candidate’s expenses referred to in any of paragraphs (4)(a) to (c).

Section.Text: Section 477.48 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.
Subsection 477.49(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Maximum expenses: postponement of polling day
Subsection.Text: If the Governor in Council orders the postponement of polling day under subsection 59(4) for an electoral district along with the corresponding extension of the election period, then the election expenses limit calculated under subsection (1) for a candidate in that electoral district is increased by adding to it the product of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the election expenses limit calculated under subsection (1) divided by the number of days in the election period before it was extended, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the number of days by which the election period is extended.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 477.55:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — entering into contracts without authorization
Section.Text: No person authorized under paragraph 477.55(c) to enter into contracts shall enter into any contract in relation to an electoral campaign other than a contract that they are authorized in writing by the official agent to enter into under that paragraph.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 477.59(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the appointment of an auditor is required under subsection 477.1(2), the auditor’s report on the return under section 477.62;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 477.59(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of litigation expenses that includes an indication of which of those expenses were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of travel and living expenses that includes an indication of which of those expenses relate to travel and sojourns during the election period;

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of accessibility expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (a.4)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of personal expenses that includes

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an indication of which of those expenses are childcare expenses,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an indication of which of those expenses are expenses referred to in paragraphs 378(1)(c) and (d), and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an indication of which of those expenses were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of electoral campaign expenses, other than the expenses referred to in paragraphs (a) to (a.4), including a statement of electoral campaign expenses incurred for voter contact calling services as defined in section 348.01, provided by a calling service provider as defined in that section, that indicates the name of that provider and the amount of those expenses;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 477.59(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Together with the electoral campaign return, the official agent of a candidate shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with documents evidencing expenses set out in the return, including bank statements, deposit slips, cancelled cheques and the candidate’s written statement referred to in subsection 477.64(1).
Paragraphs 477.64(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any travel and living expenses paid by the candidate and details of those expenses, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any litigation expenses and personal expenses paid by the candidate, details of those expenses and an indication of which of them were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: declares that the candidate did not pay for any travel and living expenses, litigation expenses or personal expenses.

Subsection.Text: Section 477.64 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
Together with the statement referred to in paragraph (1)(a), the candidate shall send their official agent documents evidencing the payment of the expenses referred to in the statement.
Section 477.64 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If, after having sent their official agent the statement referred to in subsection (1), a candidate pays a litigation expense other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1), the candidate shall, as soon as feasible after paying it,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notify their official agent of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: inform their official agent of the amount of the expense, the details of the expense and the source of the funds used to pay it; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: send their official agent documents evidencing payment of the expense.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 477.71:
No candidate shall send to their official agent a declaration referred to in paragraph 477.59(1)(d) that they know or ought reasonably to know is false or misleading.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 477.72(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — false, misleading or incomplete document

Subsection.Text: No official agent of a candidate shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with a document referred to in subsection 477.59(1), (10), (11), (12) or (15) that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the official agent knows or ought reasonably to know contains a material statement that is false or misleading; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a document referred to in paragraph 477.59(1)(a), does not substantially set out the information required under subsection 477.59(2) and, in the case of a document referred to in subsection 477.59(10), (11), (12) or (15), does not substantially set out the information required under that subsection.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Membership in House of Commons suspended — document not provided
Subsection.Text: If the Chief Electoral Officer determines, with respect to an elected candidate, that a document that was required to be provided under subsection 477.59(1), (10), (11), (12) or (15) was not provided within the period for providing it or within any extension to that period authorized under subsection 477.66(1), then, until the document is provided, the candidate is not entitled to continue to sit or vote as a member of the House of Commons as of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the expiry of the two-week period referred to in paragraph 477.68(2)(b), determined on the basis of whichever of its subparagraphs applies, if the candidate or their official agent does not apply to a judge for an order under paragraph 477.68(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the candidate or their official agent applies to a judge for an order under paragraph 477.68(1)(b), and the application is rejected, the expiry of the day on which the application is finally disposed of so as to deny it; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the candidate or their official agent applies to a judge for an order under paragraph 477.68(1)(b) and the order is granted, the expiry of the period extended by the order.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Membership in House of Commons suspended — unmade correction or revision
Subsection.Text: If the Chief Electoral Officer determines, with respect to an elected candidate, that a correction or revision authorized to be made under subsection 477.67(1) was not made within 30 days after the day on which it was authorized, then, until the correction or revision is made, the candidate is not entitled to continue to sit or vote as a member of the House of Commons as of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the expiry of the two-week period referred to in subsection 477.67(4), if the candidate or their official agent does not apply to the Chief Electoral Officer for an extension under that subsection;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the candidate or their official agent applies to the Chief Electoral Officer for an extension under subsection 477.67(4) and it is authorized, the expiry of two weeks after the expiry of the authorized extension, unless the candidate or their official agent applies to the Chief Electoral Officer for a further extension under subsection 477.67(5);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the candidate or their official agent applies to the Chief Electoral Officer for an extension under subsection 477.67(5) and it is authorized, the expiry of two weeks after the expiry of the authorized extension, unless the candidate or their official agent makes a further application to the Chief Electoral Officer for a extension under that subsection; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the candidate or their official agent applies to the Chief Electoral Officer for an extension under subsection 477.67(4) or (5), and the application is rejected, the expiry of the day on which the application is rejected.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 477.72(3)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the expiry of the two weeks referred to in paragraph 477.68(2)(a), if the candidate or their official agent does not apply to a judge for an order under paragraph 477.68(1)(a); or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 477.72(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the candidate or their official agent applies to a judge for an order under paragraph 477.68(1)(a), the expiry of the day on which the application is finally disposed of so as to deny it.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 477.72(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

As soon as an elected candidate is not entitled to continue to sit or vote as a member of the House of Commons under any of subsections (2), (2.1) and (3), the Chief Electoral Officer shall so inform the Speaker of the House of Commons.

Section.Text: The heading before section 477.73 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Subsections 477.73(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: On receipt of the certificate, the Receiver General shall pay the amount set out in it out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund to the official agent of any candidate named in the certificate as partial reimbursement for the candidate’s election expenses, travel and living expenses that are related to travel and sojourns during the election period, accessibility expenses and personal expenses. The payment may be made to the person designated by the official agent.
A candidate’s official agent shall without delay return to the Receiver General any amount received under subsection (2) that is more than the sum of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the candidate’s paid election expenses, as set out in the their electoral campaign return,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the candidate’s paid travel and living expenses that are related to travel and sojourns during the election period, as set out in their electoral campaign return,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the candidate’s paid personal expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return, other than childcare expenses or expenses referred to in paragraphs 378(1)(c) and (d),

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: 90% of the candidate’s paid childcare expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 90% of the candidate’s paid expenses referred to in paragraphs 378(1)(c) and (d), as set out in their electoral campaign return, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: 90% — to a maximum of $5,000 — of the candidate’s paid accessibility expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 477.74(1)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: states that the amount received as partial reimbursement under subsection 477.73(2) is less than the sum of the amounts calculated under paragraphs (2)(a) to (f); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the amount of the final instalment of the reimbursement of the candidate’s expenses, calculated under subsection (2).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 477.74(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The amount referred to in paragraph (1)(d) is the sum of the following, less the partial reimbursement made under section 477.73:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the candidate’s paid election expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the candidate’s paid travel and living expenses that are related to travel and sojourns during the election period, as set out in their electoral campaign return,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the candidate’s paid personal expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return, other than childcare expenses and expenses referred to in paragraphs 378(1)(c) and (d),

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: 90% of the candidate’s paid childcare expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: 90% of the candidate’s paid expenses referred to in paragraphs 378(1)(c) and (d), as set out in their electoral campaign return, and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: 90% — to a maximum of $5,000 — of the candidate’s paid accessibility expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 477.74:
The official agent shall use any amount received under subsection 477.73(2), or received under both that subsection and subsection 477.74(4), to pay to the candidate the amount that is the sum of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 60% of the candidate’s personal expenses, as set out in their electoral campaign return, other than childcare expenses and expenses referred to in paragraph 378(1)(c) or (d), that were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1), and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 90% of the total of the candidate’s childcare expenses and expenses referred to in paragraphs 378(1)(c) and (d), as set out in their electoral campaign return, that were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 477.76:

The $1,500 amount set out in paragraph 477.75(a) and the $250 amount set out in paragraph 477.75(b) shall be multiplied by the inflation adjustment factor referred to in section 384 that is in effect on polling day.
Section 477.77 of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 477.79(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the election is deemed to have been held on the day on which the writ is withdrawn or deemed to be withdrawn; and

Subsection.Text: Subsections 477.8(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The surplus amount of electoral funds that a candidate receives for an election is the amount by which the candidate’s electoral revenues referred to in subsection (3) are more than the total of the candidate’s electoral campaign expenses paid from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1) and the transfers referred to in subsection (4).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfer or sale of capital assets
Subsection.Text: Before the surplus amount of electoral funds is disposed of in accordance with sections 477.81 and 477.82, a candidate’s official agent shall either transfer any capital assets whose acquisition constitutes an electoral campaign expense within the meaning of subsection 375(1) to the registered party that has endorsed the candidate — or to the registered association of that party in the candidate’s electoral district — or sell them at their fair market value.
Paragraphs 477.8(3)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following for which the candidate was reimbursed under this Act:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an election expense,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: travel and living expenses,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an accessibility expense,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a personal expense paid from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1), and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a cost incurred in respect of a request or an application made under Part 14, if the cost was paid from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1);

Subsection.Text: Section 477.8 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
Despite subsection (3), for the purposes of this Act, none of the following is electoral revenue:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount used to pay for a candidate’s litigation expenses that was not deposited into the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount used to pay for a candidate’s personal expenses that was not deposited into the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount paid to the candidate under this Act as a reimbursement of personal expenses that were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any amount paid to the candidate under this Act as a reimbursement of a cost incurred in respect of a request or an application made under Part 14, if that cost was paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 477.46(1).

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 477.8(4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount of a reimbursement referred to in paragraph (3)(b) that the candidate transfers to that registered party; and

Section.Text: Paragraphs 477.81(3)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which they receive the final instalment of the reimbursement of the candidate’s expenses; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which the Chief Electoral Officer is provided with the candidate’s electoral campaign return, if the candidate did not receive the reimbursement mentioned in paragraph (a).

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 477.84:

Section.MarginalNote: Persons to whom this section applies

Subsection.Text: This section applies to any person who was a candidate at an election but who was not endorsed by a registered party and whose official agent disposed of the candidate’s surplus electoral funds under paragraph 477.82(b).

The official agent of a candidate who is a person referred to in subsection (1) may, for the purpose of the candidate’s electoral campaign, apply to the Chief Electoral Officer for repayment of the amount of the surplus electoral funds referred to in that subsection if that candidate

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a candidate at the general election next following the election to which the funds relate but is not endorsed by a registered party and was not a candidate at any intervening by-election;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is a candidate at one, and only one, intervening by-election and at that by-election is not endorsed by a registered party; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is a candidate at more than one intervening by-election and, at the first intervening by-election at which they are a candidate, is not endorsed by a registered party.

Subsection.Text: On receipt of a request for payment from the Chief Electoral Officer in relation to an application, the Receiver General shall pay the amount specified in the application to the candidate’s official agent out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

Section.Text: Subsection 477.9(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The candidate shall provide the statement to the Chief Electoral Officer within four months after polling day.

Section.Text: Section 478 of the Act and the heading “Interpretation” before it are repealed.
Subsection 478.61(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Nomination d’un agent membre d’une société
Subsection.Text: Tout membre d’une société nommée conformément à la présente loi à titre de vérificateur d’un parti enregistré peut être nommé agent du candidat à la direction.

Section.Text: Subsection 478.72(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: All of a leadership contestant’s financial transactions in relation to the contestant’s leadership campaign that involve the receipt of money are to be deposited to the account unless the transaction involves the receipt of the contestant’s own funds and those funds are used to pay a litigation expense or a personal expense.

All of a leadership contestant’s financial transactions in relation to the contestant’s leadership campaign that involve the payment of money are to be paid from the account unless the transaction involves the payment out of the contestant’s own funds of a litigation expense or a personal expense.
Subsection 478.73(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:
No person or entity, other than a leadership campaign agent of a leadership contestant, shall pay the contestant’s leadership campaign expenses except for

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a litigation expense;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: travel and living expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a personal expense; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a petty expense referred to in section 381.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 478.73(6) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Prohibition — paying contestant’s personal expenses, etc.
Subsection.Text: No person or entity, other than the leadership contestant or their financial agent, shall pay the contestant’s expenses referred to in any of paragraphs 4(a) to (c).
Paragraph 478.8(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of leadership contest expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of litigation expenses that includes an indication of which of those expenses were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 478.72(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them;

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of travel and living expenses;

Paragraph.Label: (a.3)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of personal expenses that includes an indication of which of those expenses were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 478.72(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them;

Paragraph.Label: (a.4)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of leadership campaign expenses, other than the expenses referred to in paragraphs (a) to (a.3);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 478.8(2)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a statement of the funds transferred by the leadership contestant to a registered party or a registered association;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 478.8(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Together with the leadership campaign return, the leadership contestant’s financial agent shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with documents evidencing expenses set out in the return, including bank statements, deposit slips, cancelled cheques and the contestant’s written statement referred to in subsection 478.85(1).
Subsection 478.8(9) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Chief Electoral Officer is deemed to have received the declaration for the purpose of section 478.931.

Section.Text: Subsection 478.83(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: As soon as feasible after the end of a leadership contest, the auditor of a leadership contestant who has accepted contributions of $10,000 or more in total or incurred leadership campaign expenses of $10,000 or more in total shall report to the contestant’s financial agent on the leadership campaign return for that contest and shall, in accordance with generally accepted auditing standards, make any examination that will enable the auditor to give an opinion in the report as to whether the return presents fairly the information contained in the financial records on which it is based.
Paragraphs 478.85(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: sets out the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any travel and living expenses paid by the contestant and details of those expenses, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any litigation expenses and personal expenses paid by the contestant, details of those expenses and an indication of which of them were paid other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 478.72(1) and the source of the funds used to pay them; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: declares that the contestant did not pay for any travel and living expenses, litigation expenses or personal expenses.

Subsection.Text: Section 478.85 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
Together with the statement referred to in paragraph (1)(a), the leadership contestant shall send their financial agent documents evidencing the payment of the expenses referred to in the statement.
Section 478.85 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If, after having sent their financial agent the statement referred to in subsection (1), a leadership contestant pays a litigation expense other than from the bank account referred to in subsection 478.72(1), the contestant shall, as soon as feasible after paying it,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: notify their financial agent of the payment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: inform their financial agent of the amount of the expense, the details of the expense and the source of the funds used to pay it; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: send their financial agent documents evidencing payment of the expense.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 478.92:
No leadership contestant shall send to their financial agent a declaration referred to in paragraph 478.8(1)(d) that they know or ought reasonably to know is false or misleading.
Section 478.93 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition — false, misleading or incomplete document
Section.Text: No financial agent of a leadership contestant shall provide the Chief Electoral Officer with a document referred to in subsection 478.8(1), (10), (11), (12) or (15) that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the financial agent knows or ought reasonably to know contains a material statement that is false or misleading; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a document referred to in paragraph 478.8(1)(a), does not substantially set out the information required under subsection 478.8(2) and, in the case of a document referred to in subsection 478.8(10), (11), (12) or (15), does not substantially set out the information required under that subsection.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 478.93:

Subsection.Text: On receipt of the documents referred to in subsection 478.8(1), including the auditor’s report, and a copy of the auditor’s invoice for the report, the Chief Electoral Officer shall provide the Receiver General with a certificate that sets out the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the expenses incurred for the audit, up to a maximum of the lesser of 3% of the leadership contestant’s leadership contest expenses and $1,500, and

Subsection.Text: On receipt of the certificate, the Receiver General shall pay the amount set out in it to the auditor out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund.

The $1,500 amount set out in paragraph (1)(a) and the $250 amount set out in paragraph (1)(b) shall be multiplied by the inflation adjustment factor referred to in section 384 that is in effect on the day on which the leadership contest ends.

Section.Text: Section 478.94 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The surplus amount of leadership campaign funds that a leadership contestant receives for a leadership contest is the amount by which the sum of the following is more than the sum of the contestant’s leadership campaign expenses paid from the bank account referred to in subsection 478.72(1) and any transfers referred to in paragraph 364(5)(b):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contributions accepted by the leadership campaign agents on behalf of the contestant;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: money received from the sale referred to in subsection (2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: amounts referred to in subsection 365(3); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other amounts received by the contestant for their leadership campaign that are required to be deposited into the bank account referred to in subsection 478.72(1) and are not repayable.

Subsection.Text: Before the surplus amount of leadership campaign funds is disposed of in accordance with sections 478.95 and 478.96, a leadership contestant’s financial agent shall sell, at their fair market value, any capital assets whose acquisition constitutes a leadership campaign expense.
Subsections 479(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:
Every election officer is responsible for maintaining order during voting hours at any place where voting takes place in accordance with Part 9 or 10.

In performing his or her duty under subsection (1) or (2), an election officer may order a person to leave the returning officer’s office or other place where the vote is taking place if the person is committing — or the officer believes on reasonable grounds that the person has committed — in the office or place an offence under this Act, any other Act of Parliament or any regulation made under any other Act of Parliament that threatens the maintenance of order.
Subsections 479(5) to (7) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If a returning officer or other election officer believes on reasonable grounds that a person has contravened paragraph 166(1)(a) or (b), the officer may cause any material that they believe on reasonable grounds was used in contravention of that paragraph to be removed from, in the case of a returning officer, his or her office or, in the case of any other election officer, the polling station.
Subsection 479(8) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every election officer has, while exercising their powers or performing their duties under this section, all the protection that a peace officer has by law.

Section.Text: Subsection 480(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who, with the intention of delaying or obstructing the electoral process, contravenes this Act, otherwise than by committing an offence under subsection (2) or section 480.1, 481, 482 or 482.1 or contravening a provision referred to in any of sections 484 to 499.

Section.Text: Section 480.1 of the Act is renumbered as subsection 480.1(1) and is amended by adding the following:

Subsection.Text: A person does not commit an offence under subsection (1) if they establish that the representation was manifestly for the purpose of parody or satire.

Section.Text: Sections 481 and 482 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every person or entity is guilty of an offence that, during an election period, distributes, transmits or publishes any material, regardless of its form, that purports to be made, distributed, transmitted or published by or under the authority of the Chief Electoral Officer, or a returning officer, political party, candidate or prospective candidate if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the person or entity was not authorized by the Chief Electoral Officer or that returning officer, political party, candidate or prospective candidate to distribute, transmit or publish it; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the person or entity distributes, transmits or publishes it with the intent of misleading the public into believing that it was made, distributed, transmitted or published by or under the authority of the Chief Electoral Officer, or that returning officer, political party, candidate or prospective candidate.

Subsection.Text: In determining whether a person or entity has committed an offence under subsection (1) the court may consider whether the material included the use of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a name, logo, social media account identifier, username or domain name that is distinctive and commonly associated with the Chief Electoral Officer, a returning officer, or the political party, candidate or prospective candidate, as the case may be; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the name, voice, image or signature of the Chief Electoral Officer, a returning officer, or the candidate or prospective candidate or of a public figure who is associated with the political party.

Subsection.Text: A person or entity does not commit an offence under subsection (1) if they establish that the material was manifestly distributed, transmitted or published for the purpose of parody or satire.
Every person or entity is guilty of an offence that, fraudulently, and with the intention of affecting the results of an election,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by means of an electro-magnetic, acoustic, mechanical or other device, intercepts or causes to be intercepted, directly or indirectly, any function of a computer system;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: uses or causes to be used, directly or indirectly, a computer system with intent to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: commit an offence under paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: destroy or alter computer data,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: render computer data meaningless, useless or ineffective,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: obstruct, interrupt or interfere with the lawful use of computer data, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: obstruct, interrupt or interfere with a person or entity in the lawful use of computer data or deny access to computer data to a person or entity that is entitled to access to it;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: uses, possesses or traffics in, or permits another person or entity to have access to, a computer password that would enable a person or entity to commit an offence under paragraph (a) or (b); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: attempts to commit any offence referred to in paragraphs (a) to (c).

Subsection.Text: Words and expressions used in subsection (1) have the same meaning as in subsection 342.1(2) of the Criminal Code.

Section.Text: Section 483 of the Act and the heading before it are repealed.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 484(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Commet une infraction l’ancien fonctionnaire électoral qui contrevient à l’alinéa 43c) (défaut de remettre des documents électoraux et du matériel électoral).
Subsection 484(2) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after paragraph (a):

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, knowingly contravenes section 39 (failure to exercise or perform the powers or duties assigned by a returning officer in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officer’s instructions); or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 484(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 23(2) (communication of information or use of personal information for unauthorized purpose);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 484(3)(e) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes paragraph 43(a) (obstruction of election officer) or knowingly contravenes paragraph 43(b) (impersonation of election officer); or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 484(3)(f) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: f)
Paragraph.Text: l’ancien fonctionnaire électoral qui contrevient sciemment à l’alinéa 43c) (défaut de remettre des documents électoraux et du matériel électoral).

Section.Text: Section 485 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Heading.TitleText: Offences under Part 4 (Register of Electors and Register of Future Electors)

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes paragraph 56(e) or (e.1) (unauthorized use of personal information recorded in Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors) is guilty of an offence.
Every person who contravenes any of paragraphs 56(a) to (d) (forbidden acts re Register of Electors or Register of Future Electors) is guilty of an offence.
Paragraphs 486(3)(c) and (d) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 91(1) (making or publishing false statement to affect election results); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes section 92 (publishing false statement of withdrawal of candidate).

Subsection.Text: Section 486 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
Every entity is guilty of an offence that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 91(1) (making or publishing false statement to affect election results); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes section 92 (publishing false statement of candidate’s withdrawal).

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 489(2)(a) to (b) of the Act are repealed.
Paragraphs 489(2)(d) and (e) of the Act are repealed.
Subsection 489(3) of the Act is amended by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (a) and by repealing paragraph (b).
Paragraphs 489(3)(c) to (g) of the Act are repealed.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 490(a.1) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, knowingly contravenes subsection 174(1) (failure to permit person to vote);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, knowingly contravenes subsection 174(2) (failure to record vote);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, contravenes any of subsections 175(1) to (3) and (5) (failure to take required measures with respect to advance polling) or subsection 176(3) (failure to cross names off list of electors) with the intention of causing the reception of a vote that should not have been cast or the non-reception of a vote that should have been cast; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: being a returning officer, contravenes subsection 176(2) (failure to cross names off list of electors) with the intention of causing the reception of a vote that should not have been cast or the non-reception of a vote that should have been cast.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 491(2) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraphs 491(3)(a) to (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: being a unit election officer, contravenes any of section 212, subsections 213(1) and (4) and 214(1) or, being an election officer, contravenes section 257 or subsection 258(3) (failure to perform duties with respect to receipt of vote) with the intention of causing the reception of a vote that should not have been cast or the non-reception of a vote that should have been cast;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, contravenes any of subsections 267(1) and (2), section 268 and subsections 269(1) and (2) (failure to perform duties re counting of the vote) with the intention of causing the reception of a vote that should not have been cast or the non-reception of a vote that should have been cast; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, contravenes any of subsection 276(1), section 277, subsections 278(1) and (3) and section 279 (failure to perform duties re counting of the vote) with the intention of causing the reception of a vote that should not have been cast or the non-reception of a vote that should have been cast.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 491:

Heading.TitleText: Offences under Part 11.1 (Prohibitions in relation to Voting)

Section.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 281.6(2) (attempting to obtain information about elector’s vote);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes any of paragraphs 281.6(3)(a) to (c) (secrecy at the poll);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 281.6(4) (secrecy — marked ballot);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes paragraph 281.7(1)(c) (ballots);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes paragraph 281.8(1)(a) (photograph or video of marked ballot);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes paragraph 281.8(1)(b) (copy of marked ballot);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes paragraph 281.8(1)(c) (distribution of photograph, video or copy of marked ballot);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes paragraph 281.9(a) or (b) (false statement);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 282(1) (person who assists elector — limit);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 282.1(1) (vouching for more than one person);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes any of paragraphs 282.1(2)(a) to (c) (vouching for person contrary to that paragraph);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 282.1(3) (vouchee acting as voucher); or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes section 282.5 (interfering with marking of ballot).

Subsection.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: being the Chief Electoral Officer, knowingly contravenes section 281.1 (voting by Chief Electoral Officer);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes section 281.2 (inducing or attempting to induce Chief Electoral Officer to vote);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes paragraph 281.3(a) (voting when not qualified);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes paragraph 281.3(b) (inducing or attempting to induce a person not qualified to vote, to vote);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes paragraph 281.4(a) (voting when not ordinarily resident in electoral district);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes paragraph 281.4(b) (inducing or attempting to induce a person who is not ordinarily resident in electoral district to vote);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 281.5(1) or (2) (voting more than once);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 281.6(1) (failure to maintain secrecy);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 281.6(5) (secrecy — counting of the votes);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes any of paragraphs 281.7(1)(a), (b) and (d) to (i) (ballots);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, contravenes paragraph 281.7(2)(a) or (b) (ballots — election officer);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: being a unit election officer, contravenes subsection 281.7(3) (special ballots — unit election officer);

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 282(2) (person who assists elector — secrecy);

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes section 282.2 (influencing electors);

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, unit election officer or member of the staff of a returning officer, knowingly contravenes section 282.3 (influencing electors);

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 282.4(1) (undue influence by foreigners);

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 282.4(4) (collusion);

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 282.4(5) (selling advertising space);

Paragraph.Label: (s)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes section 282.6 (preventing elector from voting);

Paragraph.Label: (t)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 282.7(1) (offering bribe);

Paragraph.Label: (u)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 282.7(2) (accepting bribe); or

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes paragraph 282.8(a) or (b) (intimidation, etc.).

Subsection.Text: Every entity is guilty of an offence that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 282.4(1) (undue influence by foreigners);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 282.4(4) (collusion); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 282.4(5) (selling advertising space).

Section.Text: Paragraph 492(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: being an election officer, contravenes any of sections 283 to 288 (failure to perform duties re counting of the vote) with the intention of causing the reception of a vote that should not have been cast or the non-reception of a vote that should have been cast; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 495(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: being the owner or operator of an online platform, contravenes subsection 325.1(2) or (4) (failure to publish registry of partisan advertising messages and election advertising messages for required publication period) or subsection 325.1(5) (failure to keep information for required period);

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: being a registered party, eligible party, registered association, nomination contestant, potential candidate, candidate or third party that is required to register under subsection 349.6(1) or 353(1), contravenes section 325.2 (failure to provide information about self);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 326(1) or (2) (failure to provide election survey information) or (2.1) (failure to ensure sponsor of election survey notified of date of transmission of election survey results) or, being a sponsor of an election survey, contravenes subsection 326(3) (failure to ensure report on election survey results is published); or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 495(3) of the Act is repealed.
Paragraph 495(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: being the owner or operator of an online platform, knowingly contravenes subsection 325.1(2) or (4) (failure to publish registry of partisan advertising messages and election advertising messages for required publication period) or subsection 325.1(5) (failure to keep information for required period);

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 326(1) or (2) (failure to provide election survey information) or (2.1) (failure to ensure sponsor of election survey notified of date of transmission of election survey results) or, being a sponsor of an election survey, knowingly contravenes subsection 326(3) (failure to ensure report on election survey results is published);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 495(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Every person is guilty of an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 323(1) (conducting election advertising during blackout period);

Paragraph.Label: (a.1)
Paragraph.Text: being a registered party, eligible party, registered association, nomination contestant, potential candidate, candidate or third party that is required to register under subsection 349.6(1) or 353(1), knowingly contravenes section 325.2 (failure to provide information about self); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly contravenes subsection 328(1) (causing transmission of election survey results during blackout period).

Section.Text: Subsection 495.1(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (b) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 348.16(c) (obligation to keep list of telephone numbers called).

Section.Text: Paragraphs 495.2(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 348.17(a) or 348.19(a) (obligation to keep scripts);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 348.17(b) or 348.18(a) (obligation to keep recordings); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 348.17(c), 348.18(b) or 348.19(b) (obligation to keep list of telephone numbers called).

Section.Text: The heading before section 496 of the Act is replaced by the following:
